You're home... you're finally home

[Verse 2]
The mask you wore is on the floor
You don't need it anymore
The silence has no power here
We'll banish every single fear
Just let it go, the heavy load
At the ending of this lonely road
Just lean on us, we'll hold the line
**Your broken heart will mend with time**

[Chorus]
We are your sanctuary in the storm
A fire here to keep you safe and warm
Inside this circle, held so tight
We'll be your true and gentle light
So let the painted smile fall down
You're standing on your native ground
You're home... you're finally home

[Guitar Solo]
(A soaring, melodic, and deeply emotional 80s power ballad guitar solo. It sounds like a release of breath held for four days.)

[Outro]
You're home... you're finally home
We are your sanctuary... you're not alone.
(Fades out on a warm, unresolved major chord)

title: Haven City
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
After the quiet, personal resolution of "Sanctuary," this track is the sound of pure, explosive, cathartic release.

**The Scene:**
Leo is in the backseat of Stella's car, sandwiched between Olivia and Luna. They are leaving the airport. Stella turns on the radio. The commercials end, and **this song** kicks in.

**The Sonic Shift:**
To represent Leo's internal state, the album version of this track begins with a "Fake-Out."
* **0:00 - 0:45:** It starts as a dark, heavy **Industrial** track—the lingering static of Delores's world.
* **The Break:** Suddenly, a clean, arpeggiated electric guitar riff cuts through the noise like a knife.
* **The Explosion:** The song erupts into a triumphant, unironic **Late-80s Hard Rock Anthem**. The industrial gloom vanishes, replaced by "Hair Metal" drums, soaring vocals, and the joy of a open highway.

For Leo, this isn't just a song; it is the sound of his authentic self returning. He is 19, he is safe, and he is finally, truly home.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
(The track opens with a heavy, distorted industrial drum machine and pulsing synth-bass—tense and weary.)

(Suddenly, a clean, chorus-drenched electric guitar riff cuts through the noise.)

(A massive, live drum fill kicks the song into high gear. The industrial sounds vanish instantly.)

[Verse 1]
Been on the road for a million miles
Seen a thousand tears and a thousand fake smiles
This engine's screamin', the diesel's burnin' low
Got nowhere left that I can go
I'm tired of the fightin', sick of the lies
Just wanna see my own familiar skies

[Chorus]
Oh, won't you take me home to Haven City!
Where the harbor lights are burning pretty!
Oh, won't you please just take me home, yeah!
Yeah, take me home to Haven City!
Leave the broken past for all to pity!
Oh, won't you please just take me home!

[Verse 2]
See the harbor lights up through the mist
A promise that I can't resist
Heard a kind voice on the telephone
Said 'boy, it's time that you came on home'
I'm done with darkness, done with pain
Standing out in the pouring rain

[Chorus]
Oh, won't you take me home to Haven City!
Where the harbor lights are burning pretty!
Oh, won't you please just take me home, yeah!
Yeah, take me home to Haven City!
Leave the broken past for all to pity!
Oh, won't you please just take me home!

[Guitar Solo]
(A soaring, melodic, and virtuosic late-80s hard rock guitar solo with massive bends and a triumphant, "stadium" feel.)

[Outro - Double Time]
(The tempo kicks up into a driving double-time rhythm)
Take me home!
(Oh, won't you please take me home!)
Take me home!
(To the Haven City!)
Take me home!
(Yeah!)
Take me home!
(To the harbor lights burning pretty!)
Take me home! HOME!
(Music ends with a final, crashing power chord and feedback)

title: Guiding Stars (Reprise)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The thematic epilogue of *Static & Silence*.

This track serves as a direct response to the album opener, "Nineteen and Invincible." In that first song, a naive Leo sang about his "three guiding stars" (Stella, Luna, and Olivia) with innocent optimism. Now, after surviving the industrial nightmare of Disc 1, he returns to that melody with a profound, weary gratitude.

He realizes that his sisters and best friend weren't just "guiding" him; they were the only things that kept him alive. The lyrics explicitly reference the trauma mechanisms he developed ("I learned to wear a painted smile / And walk a cold and lonely mile"), acknowledging the scars that will remain. But the music—a calm, sophisticated 80s pop arrangement featuring a soulful saxophone—signals that while the innocence is gone, the safety is permanent. He vows never to leave the Fortress again.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
There was a boy I used to be
Who saw the world so simply
Thought every road was straight and true
Beneath a sky of perfect blue
He thought that love was just a phrase
For sunny, easy, golden days
He called you all his guiding stars
Before he knew about the scars

[Chorus]
But I learned the sky can fall away
And turn a bright and sunny day to gray
And in the static and the night
You were my only guiding light
More than a feeling, more than a name
You were the fire, you were the flame
That led me through the pouring rain
And brought me back to life again

[Verse 2]
I learned to wear a painted smile
And walk a cold and lonely mile
To build a wall of industrial sound
On cold and unfamiliar ground
I learned how silence has a weight
And how a heart can fill with hate
I learned that trust can be a lie
Beneath a cold and empty sky

[Bridge]
Two stars are here, held close and tight
One shines a warm and distant light
A friendly face on a glowing screen
The miles can't ever come between
The bond we made, the truth we know
Helping a broken boy to grow

[Chorus]
And when the sky would fall away
And turn a bright and sunny day to gray
And in the static and the night
You were my only guiding light
More than a feeling, more than a name
You were the fire, you were the flame
That led me through the pouring rain
And brought me back to life again

[Instrumental Outro]
(A gentle, melodic, and deeply soulful saxophone solo plays, full of quiet reflection and gratitude. It weaves around the main melody, lingering like a long exhale, as the music slowly fades to silence.)

title: I'll Drag You Back
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
A fierce, protective power ballad sung entirely by **Luna (Isabella Croft)**.

This is the "Warrior Sister" anthem. Having nearly lost her twin brother to the silence of Delores, Luna makes a binding vow: she will never let him drift away again. The lyrics are not just a comfort; they are a declaration of intent. If the darkness comes back, she won't just call his name—she will physically **drag** him back to safety.

**The Sonic Duel:**
In a unique production choice, the track features a duel between a heavy, distorted electric guitar (representing the threat/trauma) and a piercing, aggressive **Flute** (representing Luna).

The guitar tries to dominate the mix, but the flute weaves through the distortion, matching it note-for-note in intensity and volume. It is a sonic metaphor for Luna's promise: she isn't afraid of the noise anymore. She will cut through any static to find him.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
I saw the static in your eyes
The silent screams, the practiced lies
A painted smile, a hollow shell
Trapped inside a private hell
I tried to call, I tried to warn
On that cold and broken morn
You couldn't hear my quiet plea
But you are finally back with me

[Chorus]
And I will never let you go again
This is a promise, not a "what if" or a "when"
If the darkness ever calls your name
I'll be the fire, I'll be the flame
And if I have to, with my own two hands
I'll drag you back from those broken lands

[Verse 2]
I'll be the flute song in your head
For every single word unsaid
A steady note, a gentle sound
On this new and solid ground
No more silence, no more fear
As long as I am standing here
You'll never have to walk alone
I'll bring my broken brother home

[Chorus]
And I will never let you go again
This is a promise, not a "what if" or a "when"
If the darkness ever calls your name
I'll be the fire, I'll be the flame
And if I have to, with my own two hands
I'll drag you back from those broken lands

[Instrumental Solo]
(A fierce, technical duel between the electric guitar and the flute. The guitar growls with distortion, but the flute soars above it, piercing and undeniable, ultimately claiming the final, sustained high note.)

[Chorus]
And I will never let you go again
This is a promise, not a "what if" or a "when"
If the darkness ever calls your name
I'll be the fire, I'll be the flame
And if I have to, with my own two hands
I'll drag you back from those broken lands

title: The Scar Remains
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
A mature, reflective rock track that serves as the thematic resolution of the album.

If "Sanctuary" was the relief of rescue, **"The Scar Remains"** is the hard work of recovery. Leo acknowledges that he cannot simply "reset" to the boy he was in Track 1 ("Nineteen and Invincible"). That version of him is gone, lost to the "Long Road West."

Instead of fighting this loss, he accepts it. The lyrics describe the slow, non-linear process of learning to trust again—not just his family, but his own judgment.

**The Sonic Synthesis:**
Musically, this track represents the complete integration of the band's sound. It features **Luna's Flute** (representing her emotional promise) weaving seamlessly with **Stella's New Wave Synthesizer** (representing her structural protection), both grounded by a steady, classic 80s rock beat. It is the sound of his support system becoming part of his own internal rhythm.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
I'm trying to find that boy I knew
In shades of brilliant white and blue
The one who thought he had it made
Before the colors start to fade
He's still in here, a distant ghost
The one I miss and fear the most
A part of me is lost, it's true
A space that was reserved for you... Olivia

[Chorus]
But the scars remain, a map to trace
And they have found their proper place
I'm standing on a different floor
Than I was standing on before
And I can hear a brand new sound
On this new and solid ground
The echo of a worried flute
A synthesizer's loving root

[Verse 2]
I'm learning how to trust again
To let the gentle sunlight in
I'm learning that a helping hand
Can help a broken boy to stand
I'm learning that a home can be
A fortress built for only me
By two strong women, brave and true
Who know exactly what to do

[Chorus]
'Cause the scars remain, a map to trace
And they have found their proper place
I'm standing on a different floor
Than I was standing on before
And I can hear a brand new sound
On this new and solid ground
The echo of a worried flute
A synthesizer's loving root

[Instrumental Outro]
(The flute and synthesizer trade melodies, harmonizing over the rock beat before fading into a warm, sustained chord.)

title: This House Stands
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The album's finale. A powerful, reassuring vow sung by **Stella (Beatrice Croft)**.

Throughout the album, Leo has been the one searching for safety. In this final track, Stella confirms that the search is over. She directly addresses the specific traumas Delores inflicted—the "shifting sands" of gaslighting, the "slamming doors" of isolation, and the "cage" of non-consensual touch.

She counters each trauma with a permanent architectural promise: **"This House Stands."** It is a declaration that their family unit is a fortress that will never break. She swears that he will never face abuse again, not because the world is safe, but because *she* is standing between him and the world.

**LYRICS:**

[Verse 1]
You learned to live in shifting sands
With no safe place to ever land
You learned that doors could slam and lock
Against the ticking of the clock
You learned that touch could be a cage
A quiet, simmering, bitter rage
But that old world is dead and gone
It's time to face a brand new dawn

[Chorus]
So let my arms be your new walls
To catch you every time you fall
This house stands, it will not break
For your own precious, gentle sake
If it's a hug that you should need
A simple, pure, and loving creed
Then I am here, my love is true
My arms will be a home for you

[Verse 2]
No need to brace, no need to hide
With your two sisters by your side
We'll be the lock, we'll be the key
To your own safe reality
We'll be the roof against the rain
To wash away the hurt and pain
This is a vow we'll always keep
While you are in your troubled sleep

[Guitar Solo]
(A clean, melodic, and triumphant guitar solo. It doesn't shred; it builds a steady, unbreakable wall of sound.)

[Chorus]
So let my arms be your new walls
To catch you every time you fall
This house stands, it will not break
For your own precious, gentle sake
If it's a hug that you should need
A simple, pure, and loving creed
Then I am here, my love is true
My arms will be a home for you

[Outro]
This house stands...
For you...
(Final sustained chord, resolving perfectly into silence)

title: 1996 (An Extension of Me)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# 1996 (An Extension of Me)

**LORE NOTE:**
The song establishes the backstory of Jordan's birth year, 1996, and the origins of his mother's controlling nature. Aubrie Sinclair's worldview was entirely shaped by her one sexual encounter with David Fuller, which she falsely believed would lead to marriage. After he abandoned her, she developed a deep-seated bitterness and resentment toward the world. Consequently, she views her son, Aubrey "Jordan" Fuller, not as an individual, but as her property and a literal extension of herself. This mindset drives her to keep him isolated, treating the outside world as a threat to maintain his dependence on her.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A cold, pulsing, mechanical beat begins, like a factory machine. A low, dissonant synth drone hums underneath.)

(Verse 1)
He said it was forever
That one and only night
He left me in the pieces
He was my only light
But he left a single ember
A part of him for me
A debt he'll pay forever
For all eternity

(Chorus)
(The beat becomes a pounding, rhythmic, industrial crash)
This is my son
An extension of me
My perfect boy
My property
You will never be alone
You will never be free
You are my comfort
An extension of me

(Verse 2)
The world is loud and dirty
It's full of pain and lies
I'll keep you safe forever
Just look in mother's eyes
I'll be your hands and voice
I'll be the air you breathe
You'll never have to make a choice
You'll never have to leave

(Chorus)
(Music swells, adding a grinding, metallic texture)
This is my son
An extension of me
My perfect boy
My property
You will never be alone
You will never be free
You are my comfort
An extension of me

(Bridge)
(The beat drops to a cold, rhythmic pulse)
I'll keep you here
I'll keep you pure
My only love
My only cure
You are mine
You are mine
You are mine

(Chorus)
(The music explodes to its loudest, most oppressive peak)
This is my son
An extension of me
My perfect boy
My property
You will never be alone
You will never be free
You are my comfort
An extension of me

(Outro)
(The beat fades slowly, leaving only the cold, dissonant drone)
(Whispered)
An extension of me...
Just... like... me...

title: The Dependency Loop
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Dependency Loop

**LORE NOTE:**
This track delves into the specific mechanics of Aubrie Sinclair's control over her son. She deliberately weaponized "care" by refusing to teach Jordan basic life skills, such as how to dress himself or tie his shoes, forcing him to rely entirely on her for survival. By framing the outside world as dangerous and positioning herself as his sole protector and provider, she created a suffocating "cage of comfort." The song also touches upon her medical deception—keeping him unvaccinated and isolated from doctors—ensuring her absolute authority over his physical and mental well-being to guarantee he would never leave her.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(The low drone from the previous track fades in, joined by a slow, heavy, grinding beat, like gears turning. THUD... chk... THUD... chk...)

(Verse 1)
The world outside is sharp, my love
The air is full of harm
You don't need to lift a finger
Just stay here, safe and warm
Don't you worry 'bout your buttons
Don't you worry 'bout your shoes
Mother's here to do it for you
It's the least that I can do

(Chorus)
(The beat gets heavier, more all-encompassing. A metallic clang is added.)
This is the loop, the system of care
I'll be your hands, I'll be your air
You don't need to move, you don't need to think
Just stay right here, my love, and drink
The comfort in the cage I built for you
It's all you know, it's all that's true

(Verse 2)
The doctors try to scare you
With needles and with pills
They do not know your body
They do not know your ills
I'll be your only medicine
I know just what you need
I'll keep you safe and pure, my boy
My perfect, fragile seed

(Chorus)
This is the loop, the system of care
I'll be your hands, I'll be your air
You don't need to move, you don't need to think
Just stay right here, my love, and drink
The comfort in the cage I built for you
It's all you know, it's all that's true

(Bridge)
(The music becomes more sparse, just the beat and a cold synth pad)
The bed is cold, I know
You hate to sleep alone
Just come and comfort mother
My flesh and of my bone
You're all I have, you see
You'll never be apart
You'll always be right here with me
My own dependent heart

(Guitar Solo)
(A cold, shrieking, atonal guitar solo that sounds more like a machine alarm than music)

(Chorus)
(Music crashes back in, louder and more oppressive)
This is the loop, the system of care
I'll be your hands, I'll be your air
You don't need to move, you don't need to think
Just stay right here, my love, and drink
The comfort in the cage I built for you
It's all you know, it's all that's true

(Outro)
(The grinding beat slowly fades)
It's all you know...
It's all you...

title: Inappropriate (The Opt-Out)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Inappropriate (The Opt-Out)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track explores Aubrie Sinclair's deliberate strategy to keep Jordan ignorant about human anatomy, relationships, and sexuality. By repeatedly opting him out of mandatory school health and family life classes—labeling the curriculum "inappropriate" for him—she weaponized his innocence. Her goal was to ensure he remained isolated, dependent, and entirely under her control, shielding him from any knowledge that might lead to romantic connections, personal autonomy, or an understanding of his own body. 

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A sharp, fast, staccato industrial beat. Like a typewriter, or a "REJECTED" stamp. P-CHIK. P-CHIK. P-CHIK.)

(Verse 1)
The school is full of whispers
The halls are full of filth
They teach them things I'll never say
They'll rot them all with guilt
They talk about... (spoken: ugh) ...those bodies
And the dirty things they do
But I will never let that poison
Ever get to you

(Chorus)
(The beat becomes a driving, angry pulse)
INAPPROPRIATE!
I check the box, I sign the line
INAPPROPRIATE!
Your mind will stay as pure as mine
You do not need to know those things
You'll never leave my sight
I'll keep you safe, I'll keep you clean
I'll keep you in the light

(Verse 2)
You're not like other children
You're fragile and you're small
You do not need those questions
You don't need them at all
Why talk about "relations"?
Why talk about "desire"?
It's just a fire for the weak
I'll protect you from that fire

(Chorus)
INAPPROPRIATE!
I check the box, I sign the line
INAPPROPRIATE!
Your mind will stay as pure as mine
You do not need to know those things
You'll never leave my sight
I'll keep you safe, I'll keep you clean
I'll keep you in the light

(Bridge)
(Music drops to just the sharp "stamping" beat and a harsh whisper)
Family life... (whisper: Inappropriate!)
What bodies do... (whisper: Inappropriate!)
Their changing forms... (whisper: Inappropriate!)
The books they read... (whisper: Inappropriate!)
The words they use... (whisper: Inappropriate!)
It's all...
(Music explodes)

(Chorus)
INAPPROPRIATE!
I check the box, I sign the line
INAPPROPRIATE!
Your mind will stay as pure as mine
You do not need to know those things
You'll never leave my sight
I'll keep you safe, I'll keep you clean
I'll keep you in the light

(Outro)
(The sound of a pen angrily scratching on a form, over the fading beat)
Opt-out.
Opt-out.
(A final, heavy STAMP sound)
Inappropriate.

title: The Silly College Experiment (Astrophysics)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Silly College Experiment (Astrophysics)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track explores Aubrie Sinclair's manipulative mindset regarding Jordan's enrollment at Commonwealth Polytechnic Institute. She views his desire for higher education as a "silly college experiment" and deliberately sets him up to fail by selecting Astrophysics—a highly complex and mathematically demanding major—as his degree path[cite: 1]. Her intention is not for him to succeed, but to use this "insurmountable mountain" to prove his incompetence, shatter his confidence, and force him to return home, cementing his lifelong dependency on her[cite: 1].

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A slow, grinding industrial beat, similar to "Dependency Loop," but with a new, mocking, high-pitched synth melody, like a warped nursery rhyme.)

(Verse 1)
You want to go to college
You want to play pretend
This silly little game of yours
I know just how it ends
You think you're smart, my Aubrey
You think you'll get away
But I know how to break you
I know the words to say

(Chorus)
(The beat becomes heavier, more deliberate and cruel)
So choose the stars, my little one
Choose the great unknown
A world of math you'll never pass
Out there, all alone
It's far too hard, it's far too cold
A trap I've set for you
This "silly college experiment"
Will be your Waterloo

(Verse 2)
You'll fail your first assignment
You'll panic at the tests
You'll realize you're not like them
You're not as good as them
You'll see you're just a burden
Incapable and weak
You'll call me, crying, begging
You won't last a single week

(Chorus)
So choose the stars, my little one
Choose the great unknown
A world of math you'll never pass
Out there, all alone
It's far too hard, it's far too cold
A trap I've set for you
This "silly college experiment"
Will be your Waterloo

(Bridge)
(Music drops to just a buzzing, electrical drone)
(Whispered)
Run along. Play your game.
It's all part of the plan.
You'll come back home.
You'll come back to me.
They always come back to me...
(The grinding beat crashes back in)

(Chorus)
So choose the stars, my little one
Choose the great unknown
A world of math you'll never pass
Out there, all alone
It's far too hard, it's far too cold
A trap I've set for you
This "silly college experiment"
Will be your Waterloo

(Outro)
(The "warped nursery rhyme" synth melody fades out)
You'll come home...
You'll fail...
You'll come home...

title: Route Four-Sixty (The Seven-Hour Drive)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Route Four-Sixty (The Seven-Hour Drive)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track captures the tense, seven-hour journey from the humid flatlands of Hampton Roads to the rolling mountains of the New River Valley for college move-in day. Traveling westward along U.S. Route 460, Jordan is trapped in the passenger seat, forced to endure a relentless stream of anxious instructions and oppressive rules from his mother, Aubrie Sinclair. Instead of feeling the excitement of a new beginning, he feels like a prisoner being transported to an academic jail, overwhelmed by panic and the suffocating fear that he is destined to fail her "silly college experiment."

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A fast, pulsing, anxious beat, like a rapid heartbeat. The sound of a car engine idling, distorted and rhythmic. A high, whining synth note, like a ringing in the ears.)

(Verse 1)
Seven hours of silence
Seven hours of her voice
A list of rules and warnings
I didn't have a choice
The mountains rolling past the glass
A world I'll never know
I'm just a passenger, I'm trapped
With nowhere else to go

(Chorus)
(The beat becomes a fast, driving, industrial chug, like a runaway train)
Route four-sixty, rolling west
A knot of panic in my chest
The engine is a pounding drum
From everything I've overcome
...Or everything I'm running to
I don't know what I'm going to do
This silly college experiment
I'll break before I'm bent

(Verse 2)
She's watching me, I feel her eyes
She thinks I'm going to fail
This trap she set, this "college" test
A cage outside the jail
The road is long, my hands are cold
I wish that I could hide
This car's a cell, this angry hum
With her right here inside

(Chorus)
Route four-sixty, rolling west
A knot of panic in my chest
The engine is a pounding drum
From everything I've overcome
...Or everything I'm running to
I don't know what I'm going to do
This silly college experiment
I'll break before I'm bent

(Bridge)
(The music drops to just the anxious, high-pitched ringing and the pulsing heartbeat)
I'm not ready...
I'm not ready...
She's right, I'll fail
I'm not ready...
(The chugging beat slams back in)

(Chorus)
Route four-sixty, rolling west
A knot of panic in my chest
The engine is a pounding drum
From everything I've overcome
...Or everything I'm running to
I don't know what I'm going to do
This silly college experiment
I'll break before I'm bent

(Outro)
(The sound of the chugging beat and the engine noise slowly fades, leaving only the anxious, high-pitched ringing)

title: Stranded (A Real College Kid)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Stranded (A Real College Kid)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track captures Jordan's profound isolation and despair during his first night alone in his dorm room at CPI. After his mother departs with the hollow praise of him being a "real college kid" and "living on your own," the stark reality sets in: she deliberately never taught him fundamental life skills. Unable to reach a wall outlet, change his own clothes, or even tie his shoes, Jordan realizes he is completely stranded in his own space. The song reflects his crushing realization that her words weren't a genuine statement of pride, but rather a cruel confirmation that she had set him up to fail and was glad to be rid of the burden.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A cold, ambient, synth pad. The sound of wind, or a distant, empty machine. No beat, just atmosphere.)

(Verse 1)
The door is closed, the lock is set
The first time I'm alone
This quiet is a heavy sheet
On this bed that's not my own
My laptop's dark, the plug's too high
A world I cannot reach
A simple task I cannot do
A lesson she won't teach

(Chorus)
(A faint, slow, rhythmic pulse enters, like a distant heartbeat. The vocals are a scared, internal whisper.)
"A real college kid"
That's what she said to me
"An adult, living on your own"
But I can't even get free
I'm stranded in these denim clothes
My shoes are on the floor
I'm "a real college kid"
And I can't find the door

(Verse 2)
The rain begins to tap the glass
A cold and lonely sound
I have a bag of sandwiches
I'm trapped here on my own
She never taught me how to change
She never taught me how to tie
She never really taught me
How to say goodbye

(Chorus)
"A real college kid"
That's what she said to me
"An adult, living on your own"
But I can't even get free
I'm stranded in these denim clothes
My shoes are on the floor
I'm "a real college kid"
And I can't find the door

(Bridge)
(The music swells slightly with a dissonant, cold pad)
What if she was right?
What if I just fail?
This "silly college experiment"
This academic jail...
I want to go home
(Whispered) I'm not ready...
(Whispered) I'm not...

(Chorus)
"A real college kid"
That's what she said to me
"An adult, living on your own"
But I can't even get free
I'm stranded in these denim clothes
My shoes are on the floor
I'm "a real college kid"
And I can't find the door

(Outro)
(The cold, ambient drone and the faint pulse fade to silence)
Living on my own...
Living on my own...
Alone...

title: Clerical Error (A New Light)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Clerical Error (A New Light)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track marks the pivotal turning point in Jordan's life. Due to a university housing crisis and a clerical error that mistook his legal name, "Aubrey," for a female, he is assigned to a handicap-accessible suite with five young women. On official move-in day, the cacophony of the hallway and the sudden realization of the mix-up trigger a severe autistic meltdown. Rather than reacting with judgment or fear, his suitemate Aubrie Jean Kelly—who is also autistic—immediately recognizes his distress. She steps forward to ground him, wrapping him in a firm, protective embrace. This profound act of unconditional kindness is the very first he has ever experienced, sparking a "new light" in his dark world and laying the foundation for their extraordinary chosen family.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(The cold, empty, ambient drone from the previous track, "Stranded," fades in... and then abruptly cuts to silence.)
(A single, clean, melodic piano motif begins, playing softly. It sounds hopeful, curious, and a little sad.)

(Verse 1)
(Sung softly, almost spoken)
Open the door, room two four teen
My arms are full of bags
But I'm not the first one here
Someone's in the bed
His pillows and his blankets
He's already fast asleep
I must have the wrong room
My heart begins to leap...

(Verse 2)
(The piano continues, simple and clear)
I check the card upon the door
My stomach's in a knot
"Aubrie Jean Kelly"
"Madison Elizabeth Scott"
And "Aubrey Jordan Fuller"
A clerical mistake
The boy in bed begins to stir
He's starting to awake...

(Chorus)
(Warm strings swell gently under the piano)
He sits up, and I see his eyes
And I'm not scared at all
He just looks lost and terrified
He looks so... small
My mind just goes completely clear
A feeling I can't place
It's the first time that I've ever seen
Such kindness in a face
A new light switching on...

(Verse 3)
(The music becomes a little more anxious as the 'meltdown' begins)
The noise outside the hallway
Is suddenly too much
He's rocking in his wheelchair
He's trembling at my touch
(Spoken, softly) "He's not angry. He's overwhelmed."
I know just what he needs
He needs a quiet anchor
A place where he can breathe

(Chorus)
(The music swells to a warm, emotional climax)
He sits up, and I see his eyes
And I'm not scared at all
He just looks lost and terrified
He looks so... small
My mind just goes completely clear
This feeling I can't place
It's the first time that I've ever seen
Such kindness in a face
A new light switching on...

(Outro)
(The piano motif plays, fading slowly)
(Whispered)
"It's okay. Focus on my voice."
"You're safe."
A new light...

title: System.Crash(Meltdown_01)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# System.Crash(Meltdown_01)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track thrusts the listener directly into the chaotic sensory overload of Jordan's first major autistic meltdown in the story. Triggered by the deafening noise of freshman move-in day, the sudden presence of female strangers in his suite, and the terrifying realization of the housing mix-up over his legal name "Aubrey," his mind experiences a complete system failure. Unable to process the compounding stressors or execute an escape, he is paralyzed by panic, rocking in his wheelchair and covering his ears as his world temporarily shatters around him.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A high, anxious synth drone and the sound of the 'Clerical Error' piano motif, but it's immediately crushed by a LOUD, pounding, harsh industrial beat.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are fast, panicked, and rhythmic)
TOO LOUD! TOO BRIGHT!
TOO MANY! WRONG!
THIS ISN'T RIGHT!
THIS ISN'T RIGHT!
MY ROOM! MY NAME!
THE NOISE! THE NOISE!
THE SYSTEM'S CRASHED!

(Chorus)
CAN'T BREATHE! CAN'T THINK!
GOTTA GET OUT! I'M ON THE BRINK!
IT'S ALL WRONG! IT'S ALL WRONG!
THIS ISN'T ME!
THIS ISN'T ME!

(Verse 2)
(The beat gets faster, more frantic)
THEY'RE STARING!
THEY'RE WATCHING!
A MISTAKE! I'M THE MISTAKE!
HAVE TO MOVE! HAVE TO PACK!
CAN'T DO IT AGAIN!
THE NOISE!

(Chorus)
CAN'T BREATHE! CAN'T THINK!
GOTTA GET OUT! I'M ON THE BRINK!
IT'S ALL WRONG! IT'S ALL WRONG!
THIS ISN'T ME!
THIS ISN'T ME!

(Bridge)
(Music drops to just a frantic heartbeat pulse and his panted breathing)
(Whispered)
It's too much... it's too much... it's too much...
(A high-pitched feedback whine builds)
(Screamed)
IT'S TOO MUCH!

(Outro)
(The full industrial beat slams back in for one final, chaotic bar and then cuts abruptly to silence.)

title: The Anchor Holds (Aubrie's Theme)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Anchor Holds (Aubrie's Theme)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track shifts the perspective to Aubrie Jean Kelly during the chaotic events of move-in day. Being autistic herself, Aubrie instantly recognizes that Jordan's frantic rocking and covering of his ears is not an act of aggression, but a severe sensory and emotional overload. Gently explaining to her parents that he is simply overwhelmed, she steps into the room and takes control of the situation. By calmly asking for his consent and providing a firm, grounding embrace, she acts as a living anchor for his crashing system. This profound, unconditional act of care marks the very first genuine hug Jordan has ever received in his life, cementing Aubrie's role as his primary safe harbor.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)

(Verse 1)
(Spoken)
"He's not angry. He's overwhelmed."
(Sung)
I see the signs, I know this pain
The static flooding in your brain
It's not a choice, it's not a flaw
It's just the world, and all its raw
Input...

(Chorus 1)
It's okay. You're safe.
Focus on my voice.
This isn't a trap, you have a choice.
Just breathe...

(Bridge)
(Spoken)
"Aubrey. My name is Aubrie."
"Is it okay if I put my hand on your shoulder?"
"Is it okay... if I hug you?"

(Chorus 2)
I'm here. I've got you.
You're not alone.
The storm is passed, you're safe, you're home.
The first unconditional...
The first real hug...
You're safe.

(Outro)
You're safe...

title: The Same Constellations
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Same Constellations

**LORE NOTE:**
Following the chaotic events of move-in day and Jordan's subsequent meltdown, he and Aubrie share a quiet moment of connection. Jordan nervously reveals his assigned major, Astrophysics—a subject his mother deliberately chose as an insurmountable trap to ensure he would fail her "silly college experiment." He fully expects Aubrie to pity him or confirm his inadequacy. Instead, her face lights up as she joyfully declares, "Me too!" For Aubrie, the cosmos and the laws of physics are a logical, predictable sanctuary from a confusing world. Her genuine excitement shatters his mother's manipulative trap, transforming his greatest academic fear into a shared passion and a fragile, dawning hope for the future.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are soft, hesitant, and full of wonder)
I'm sitting in the quiet
The storm has passed me by
She's holding me and breathing
And I don't have to cry
And in the peaceful after
I hear her ask my name
I say, "It's complicated"
She says, "Mine's the same."

(Verse 2)
She asks me what I'm taking
I wait for her to laugh
I tell her "Astrophysics"
That cold and broken path
My mother's "silly experiment"
The trap to make me fail
The math I'll never navigate
The wind without a sail

(Chorus)
But she just smiles and says "Me too!"
And my whole world just... stops
The pieces of my broken life
Are falling into slots
She doesn't see a failure
She doesn't see a trap
She's pointing to the patterns
On a different kind of map

(Bridge)
(Music is just piano and voice)
She talks about the "logic"
A "sanctuary" from the noise
A place where all the chaos
Follows a single voice
She's speaking in my language
A language I forgot
She's not a part of mother's plan
She's something she could not...

(Chorus)
And she just smiles and says "Me too!"
And my whole world just... stops
The pieces of my broken life
Are falling into slots
She doesn't see a failure
She doesn't see a trap
She's pointing to the patterns
Of the same constellations on our map

(Outro)
The same constellations...
She sees them too...
Me too...

title: The Naming (A Joke That Stuck)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Naming (A Joke That Stuck)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track represents a crucial moment of empowerment and self-determination for the protagonist. During a quiet conversation with Aubrie Jean Kelly, he explains the origin of his legal first name, "Aubrey." His abusive mother, whose name is Aubrie, gave him the gender-neutral name as a cruel joke that ultimately became permanent on his birth certificate. Recognizing the shared name as a source of his lifelong trauma, daily confusion, and the catalyst for the dorm room mix-up, he makes his first true act of defiance. He announces that he despises the name "Aubrey" and chooses to go by his middle name, "Jordan," officially shedding his old identity and stepping into his new life.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are soft, hesitant, and full of wonder)
She breaks the quiet, asks my name
A nervous laugh, a world of shame
"It's Aubrey, just the same as you"
"But spelled a different way, it's true"
She doesn't flinch, she doesn't run
This simple kindness has begun...

(Pre-Chorus)
(Music builds slightly)
I have to tell her, have to say
The reason I was named this way
"My mother's name is Aubrie, too"
"It's something that she thought to do..."

(Chorus)
(The full piano-rock band kicks in, confident and mid-tempo)
It was a joke that just kind of stuck
A name I've cursed, my rotten luck
A label that she put on me
That I was never meant to be
But looking at her, I can see
A chance to change my history

(Bridge)
(Vocals are firm, a clear decision)
This is my chance, this is the day
I'll be the one to walk away
From "Aubrey" and the life he led
The name she used inside my head
This is my first act of defiance
My declaration of alliance...

(Chorus)
(Music is strong and proud)
It was a joke that just kind of stuck
But now I'm making my own luck
I'm shedding skin I've worn too long
A place where I could not belong
You can call me Jordan
Yeah, you can call me Jordan
From now on...
Call me Jordan.

(Outro)
Call me Jordan...

title: A Different Kind of Constellation (The Pact)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# A Different Kind of Constellation (The Pact)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track details the radical and beautiful formation of the group's poly-affectionate dynamic. After Lauren is publicly humiliated and cruelly dumped by her ex-boyfriend, Jordan steps in to comfort her, offering her unconditional tenderness and a safe embrace. Watching this, Aubrie Jean realizes she feels absolutely no jealousy—only a profound sense of rightness and protective care. She proposes an unconventional solution: rather than competing for Jordan and treating him like a prize to be won, the single women of the suite (Aubrie, Madison, Zoe, and Lauren) agree to share his romantic affection as co-equals. Hannah, devoted to her boyfriend Mark, lovingly opts out of the romantic aspect but cements her role as his platonic best friend and official hug-giver. They cease to be roommates and become a "constellation" of support, fundamentally defining the open-hearted terms of Jordan's new life.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A strong, clear piano melody begins, joined quickly by a mid-tempo, driving rock beat and a warm, clean electric guitar.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are warm, clear, and full of conviction)
I see you crying on the bed
The poison he put in your head
He made you feel so small and cheap
A promise that he couldn't keep
But then I see you look at him
Our Jordan, on a sudden whim
He holds you, and you're finally safe
He's the antidote, he's the escape

(Pre-Chorus)
(Music builds, strings swell)
I see the way that Madi smiles
I see the way that Zoe stares
We all can see his kindness
And how much he truly cares
The old-world rules would make us fight
Would make us choose and turn away
But I have something different
I have something new to say

(Chorus)
(Music explodes into a warm, powerful, piano-rock anthem)
Why must love be a competition?
A single prize, a selfish war?
It's not a game of possession
It's something so much more
I don't feel any jealousy
I only feel what's true
If he's the one who's healing you
Then he belongs to you, too
We're a different kind of constellation
A new design, a brand new light
No jealousy. Just care.
Tonight.

(Verse 2)
You're hurting, and you need someone
Who's good and kind and clear
He's sitting right beside you
He is right here
I have a massive crush on him
I'll say it, it's okay
But it doesn't make me angry
To see you feel this way

(Pre-Chorus)
I see the way that Madi smiles
I see the way that Zoe stares
We all can see his kindness
And how much he truly cares
The old-world rules would make us fight
But I am not afraid
Of this new, loving system
That we have made

(Chorus)
Why must love be a competition?
A single prize, a selfish war?
It's not a game of possession
It's something so much more
I don't feel any jealousy
I only feel what's true
If he's the one who's healing you
Then he belongs to you, too
We're a different kind of constellation
A new design, a brand new light
No jealousy. Just care.
Tonight.

(Outro)
(The piano and strings swell and then fade)
No jealousy.
Just care.
Tonight.

title: The Cut Cord (No Service)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Cut Cord (No Service)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track depicts Aubrie Sinclair's vindictive retaliation after Jordan refuses to leave his new family and return home for Fall Break. Having been denied physical access to him at the dorm, she executes a calculated strike to isolate him by terminating his cell phone service. Her goal is to cut his digital cord to the outside world, punishing his independence and attempting to prove that he cannot survive without her financial control and authority. She believes this forced isolation will make him come crawling back. 

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A cold, pulsing, electronic beat, like a "No Service" signal. The sound of a phone off the hook, distorted and rhythmic. A high, whining synth drone.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are cold, spiteful, and processed)
You stood against me
You chose their side
You think you're free now
You think you can hide
You think those... girls...
Can keep you safe?
You just embarrassed me
You're a disgrace

(Pre-Chorus)
You forgot who holds the strings
You forgot who pays the bills
You think you have a voice, my boy?
I'll show you what "control" still builds
You are an adult? (scoffs)
Let's see how long you last

(Chorus)
(The beat becomes a heavy, deliberate, industrial STOMP)
CLICK.
The line is dead. The signal's gone.
I've cut the cord, it won't be long
'Til you come crawling back to me
How will you talk, boy, when you're not free?
No service. No connection.
Just me.

(Verse 2)
You're just a child
You're weak and you're slow
You can't survive out there
You have nowhere to go
They'll get tired of you
They'll see the burden
And when they throw you out
I'll be the one to let you in

(Chorus)
CLICK.
The line is dead. The signal's gone.
I've cut the cord, it won't be long
'Til you come crawling back to me
How will you talk, boy, when you're not free?
No service. No connection.
Just me.

(Bridge)
(Music drops to just the cold pulse and a harsh whisper)
"Your call cannot be completed as dialed."
"Please hang up and try again."
But there's no one to call.
There's no one there.
You're all alone, Aubrey.
You're all alone...

(Chorus)
(Music explodes back in)
CLICK.
The line is dead. The signal's gone.
I've cut the cord, it won't be long
'Til you come crawling back to me
How will you talk, boy, when you're not free?
No service. No connection.
Just me.

(Outro)
(The beat fades, leaving only the sound of a distorted, off-hook phone signal)
No service...
No service...

title: [LOGISTICS] (The Buddy System)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# [LOGISTICS] (The Buddy System)

**LORE NOTE:**
When Aubrie Sinclair cuts off Jordan's cellular service in a malicious attempt to isolate him, his chosen family immediately counters with a brilliant logistical defense. Zoe utilizes a VoIP app to secure a new, private phone number for Jordan that operates entirely over the university's WiFi, effectively bypassing his mother's financial control. To protect him when he is between buildings and off the network, Madison creates the "Jordan Comms" group chat and institutes a strict "Buddy System," ensuring he is never without a trusted family member by his side. Musically, this track is a turning point: it introduces bright, hopeful 80s synth-rock elements that actively push back against the dark, industrial tones of his past, symbolizing his new family's network successfully defeating his mother's oppressive control.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A fast, driving, hopeful beat. A bright, 80s-style synthesizer arpeggio plays over the pact's "Piano-Rock" theme. The sound of a system booting up.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are fast, analytical, and confident)
She cut the line. A single thread.
A petty move, a sign of dread.
She thinks a single point of failure
Will be enough to break our wall
But she fails to see the logic
A single node is prone to fall

(Pre-Chorus)
A single line is weak and small
We won't be beaten by her spite
We'll build a brand new system
We'll build the net tonight

(Chorus)
(Music swells, triumphant and fast)
[LOGISTICS] A new connection!
A group chat for his protection!
A new VoIP, a brand new line
A "Never Alone" protocol design!
She can't cut a cord when we're a NET
A web of care she can't upset
We are the system, we are the shield
Her petty malice has to yield!

(Verse 2)
A buddy, always at his side
No place for him to run or hide
(Spoken) Not from us, from her.
(Sung)
A handoff, clean, without a flaw
A digital, loving, social law
A web of care she can't unweave
A fortress she cannot perceive

(Pre-Chorus)
A single line is weak and small
We won't be beaten by her spite
We'll build a brand new system
We'll build the net tonight!

(Chorus)
[LOGISTICS] A new connection!
A group chat for his protection!
A new VoIP, a brand new line
A "Never Alone" protocol design!
She can't cut a cord when we're a NET
A web of care she can't upset
We are the system, we are the shield
Her petty malice has to yield!

(Bridge)
(Music swells, very 80s-inspired)
She thinks in lines, we think in code
She thinks in spite, we share the load
She built a cage, we built the key
A FIREWALL OF FAMILY!

(Guitar & Synth Solo)

(Chorus)
[LOGISTICS] A new connection!
A group chat for his protection!
A new VoIP, a brand new line
A "Never Alone" protocol design!
She can't cut a cord when we're a NET
A web of care she can't upset
We are the system, we are the shield
Her petty malice has to yield!

(Outro)
(The fast synth arpeggio fades)
(Spoken)
"[LOGISTICS] Handoff complete."
"[LOGISTICS] System is stable."
"[LOGISTICS] He is safe."

title: Sanctuary (The Thanksgiving Exodus)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Sanctuary (The Thanksgiving Exodus)

**LORE NOTE:**
When Aubrie Sinclair sends a Thanksgiving ultimatum demanding Jordan return to Hampton Roads, his chosen family orchestrates a swift escape. Utilizing the Maroon Express, the group travels to Christiansburg, Virginia, to seek refuge at the home of Zoe's family, the Brooks. Although it is just a short bus ride down the road, to Jordan, the journey feels like an exodus deep into the Appalachian mountains, far beyond his mother's oppressive grip. Upon arriving at the house on a hill, he is met with immediate, unconditional acceptance. Laura Brooks, Zoe's mother, doesn't see a burden or a broken child; she simply sees her daughter's friend—her "daughter's boy." For the first time in his life, Jordan experiences the warmth and safety of a true family home, cementing the Brooks' residence as his ultimate sanctuary.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A warm, driving, acoustic guitar riff begins, joined by a simple, solid 4/4 rock drum beat and a gentle harmonica.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are hopeful, relieved, but still a little fragile)
The Maroon Express is rolling fast
Our shadows on the window glass
We're leaving campus in the dark
A single, hopeful, burning spark
The eight of us, a single pack
We're never, ever going back

(Verse 2)
We're leaving just a day too soon
Beneath a cold November moon
My mother's cold ultimatum
Is just a fading, dying sound
We're heading to a brand new place
A quiet, safe, and solid ground

(Chorus)
(Music swells, adds a simple, clean electric guitar melody)
This is a sanctuary
A house built on a hill
A place where I can finally breathe
A place where I can just be still
They're not just friends, I'm starting to see
They're my chosen family
This basement is my sanctuary

(Bridge)
(Music is a little quieter, focus on the acoustic guitar and voice)
Laura's smiling at the door
She's happy that we're here
She doesn't see a burden
She doesn't show a trace of fear
A full house for Thanksgiving
She says with so much joy
She doesn't see a broken thing
She just sees... her daughter's boy

(Chorus)
(Music is full, warm, and driving)
This is a sanctuary
A house built on a hill
A place where I can finally breathe
A place where I can just be still
They're not just friends, I'm starting to see
They're my chosen family
This basement is my sanctuary

(Outro)
(Acoustic guitar and harmonica riff fades out)
My sanctuary...
We're safe...
We're home...

title: Guardianship (The Summons)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Guardianship (The Summons)

**LORE NOTE:**
Back at CPI for the Spring semester, Jordan's hard-won peace is violently interrupted when he receives an ominous email summoning him alone to Hall Director Kelsey's office at 3:00 PM. Waiting inside is a Montgomery County Sheriff's Deputy. When the uniformed officer formally addresses him by his full legal name, "Aubrey Jordan Fuller," the trauma of his mother's abuse and the terrifying realization of her reach triggers a severe autistic meltdown. Kelsey expertly steps in to de-escalate the situation, but the damage is done. The envelope contains a court summons: Aubrie Sinclair has filed a petition in Norfolk to have Jordan declared legally incompetent and placed under her guardianship, launching a desperate, malicious legal war to drag him back under her control.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A low, ominous drone. A single, heavy, distorted bass note hits. A slow, crushing, industrial beat begins, like a massive hammer.)

(Verse 1)
(Male vocals are a terrified, choked whisper)
Kelsey's office. Three o'clock.
She said to come alone.
The door is shut. The air is thick.
A man in a uniform.
My blood runs cold. My hands go numb.
I can't feel my own chair.
He's holding out a folded sheet...
This isn't real. This isn't fair.

(Pre-Chorus)
(The beat gets heavier, more rhythmic)
He looks at me, his face is grim
He starts to read the line
He doesn't know the words he holds
Are a trigger to my mind...

(Chorus)
(Music explodes. A full-on, crushing, industrial assault. Vocals are a pained, rhythmic SHOUT.)
(Spoken, distorted official voice:)
"Are you Aubrey Jordan Fuller?"
(Male Vocal)
SYSTEM CRASH!
THAT IS NOT MY NAME!
SYSTEM CRASH!
SHE'S WON THE GAME!
HE'S HERE! SHE'S WON!
THE WALLS ARE GONE!
(A low, humming, guttural sound)
NO! NO! NO! NO!

(Verse 2)
(Vocals are a fragmented, non-verbal, panicked rhythm over the beat)
Can't breathe! Can't see!
Rocking! Shaking!
The lights are bright!
The world is breaking!
He's just a man...
A uniform...
He's just... her...
He's her in here!

(Chorus)
(Spoken, distorted official voice:)
"Are you Aubrey Jordan Fuller?"
(Male Vocal)
SYSTEM CRASH!
THAT IS NOT MY NAME!
SYSTEM CRASH!
SHE'S WON THE GAME!
HE'S HERE! SHE'S WON!
THE WALLS ARE GONE!
(A low, humming, guttural sound)
NO! NO! NO! NO!

(Outro)
(The crushing beat hammers, then stops. A high-pitched, electronic "flatline" drone.)
(Whispered)
...gone...
...not me...
...not me...

title: The Bluewater Bunker
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Bluewater Bunker

**LORE NOTE:**
This track details the group's frantic flight from Blacksburg to Bluewater, Virginia—a fictional town located near the real-world interchange of I-77 and US-58—after Aubrie Sinclair's private investigator discovers their location at the Hotel at Poly. Taking refuge in Madison Scott's childhood home, the group creates the "Bluewater Bunker." While the house provides a necessary safe haven from the looming court case and the immediate threat of the PI, it presents a grueling physical challenge for Jordan. The inaccessible environment forces him to endure humiliating physical struggles just to navigate the space. However, anchored by the presence of his "safe people," Aubrie and Madison, the temporary fortress becomes a place of profound emotional security despite the overwhelming physical hardships and anxiety.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A sparse, minimalist piano line, with a faint, anxious, dissonant string pad underneath. It sounds quiet, but tense.)

(Verse 1)
(Male vocals are a quiet, strained whisper)
The Pea Eye found us at the hotel
The fortress fell, the sanctuary's breached
A frantic call, a bug-out plan
A safe-house just outside her reach
The convoy rolled, a silent thread
To Bluewater, to hide instead

(Verse 2)
Her childhood room, a tiny space
An inaccessible, cramped place
The doorway is too narrow
I can't get through the bathroom door
I crawl upon the hardwood floor
My spirit feeling bruised and sore

(Chorus)
(Music swells slightly, but remains anxious)
It's a hell of inconvenience
A fragile, temporary defense
But I am safe here, hiding out
I kill the fear, I kill the doubt
This is the Bluewater Bunker
And I am here with you
My "safe person," you'll see me through

(Verse 3)
(The piano becomes more repetitive, obsessive)
The nights are long, the bed is small
(It's just a pool raft, after all)
I lie awake, I'm terrified
Of what's to come, with nowhere left to hide
The court is looming, dark and deep
I cannot rest, I cannot sleep

(Chorus)
It's a hell of inconvenience
A fragile, temporary defense
But I am safe here, hiding out
I kill the fear, I kill the doubt
This is the Bluewater Bunker
And I am here with you
My "safe person," you'll see me through

(Bridge)
(The music drops to just a single, pulsing piano note)
But in the dark, you take my hand
You're the one who'll understand
You and Aubrie, here with me
My anchors in this angry sea
The house is small, the fear is real
But your love is what I feel

(Outro)
(The sparse piano fades out)
Safe... in the Bluewater Bunker.
Safe...

title: Dismissed (With Prejudice)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Dismissed (With Prejudice)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track serves as the triumphant climax to the first half of Jordan's journey, detailing his definitive victory in a Norfolk courtroom. Facing his abusive mother and her sleazebag lawyer, Jordan is backed by an unshakeable wall of sixteen family members filling the gallery. His lawyer, Ms. Gable, systematically dismantles the petition for guardianship and conservatorship not with emotion, but with undeniable facts: Jordan's Dean's List transcript, his independently filed tax returns, and his proactive healthcare decisions. After Jordan testifies to his own safety and independence, the furious judge reprimands his mother for wasting the court's time and dismisses the case "with prejudice." The ruling permanently severs Aubrie Sinclair's legal reach, granting Jordan absolute freedom.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)

(Verse 1)
I'm in a courtroom, cold and gray
She's on the left side, across the way
Her lawyer's spinning twisted lies
About the "boy" he's "incapacitated"
A "captive" who's been "manipulated"
But then my shark begins to rise...

(Pre-Chorus)
She stands and speaks, her voice is clear
"Your Honor, there's no captive here"
"He's a competent adult"
"With a four point oh result!"

(Chorus)
DEAN'S LIST! A transcript in her hand!
SELF-ADVOCACY! He took a stand!
A wall of facts, a wall of proof
It's tearing off her broken roof!
And in the back, my family
Sixteen strong, all here for me
This isn't a fight, it's a release
She has no case!

(Verse 2)
The judge's eyes, they turn to me
"Son, do you feel you are not free?"
I look at her, my voice is strong
"I'm safer than I've ever been"
"This is the only family"
"That I have ever known or seen!"

(Chorus)
DEAN'S LIST! A transcript in her hand!
SELF-ADVOCACY! He took a stand!
A wall of facts, a wall of proof
It's tearing off her broken roof!
And in the back, my family
Sixteen strong, all here for me
This isn't a fight, it's a release
She has no case!

(Bridge)
(Spoken - Female Vocal)
"This is not a case of incapacity."
"This is a parent who can't let go."
"This petition is a waste of this court's time!"
"You are dangerously close to legal harassment."
"Dismissed! With prejudice!"

(Guitar & Piano Solo)

(Chorus)
DEAN'S LIST! A transcript in her hand!
SELF-ADVOCACY! He took a stand!
A wall of facts, a wall of proof
It's tearing off her broken roof!
And in the back, my family
Sixteen strong, all here for me
This isn't a fight, it's a release
She has no case!

(Outro)
Dismissed!
Dismissed!
With prejudice!

title: The New Normal (6:00 AM)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The New Normal (6:00 AM)

**LORE NOTE:**
Following their move to the Brooks' family home in Christiansburg, Zoe initially devises a highly efficient but physically punishing commute schedule. By putting the group on the 5:45 AM Maroon Express, they beat the morning rush, but are left waiting 45 minutes in the freezing cold before the CPI campus library opens. Recognizing the severe health risk this environmental exposure poses to Jordan's medically vulnerable system, Zoe abandons her initial plan and recalculates. She determines that the 6:00 AM Blue Ridge Transit Route 1 local bus is the optimal, safe alternative—trading a longer, 45-minute ride for a vastly reduced 15-minute wait in the cold. The song is a fast, analytical, and triumphant track representing Zoe's logical system rightfully prioritizing Jordan's well-being over pure efficiency.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)

(Verse 1)
The 5:45 Maroon Express
Was a logistical mistake
A 30-minute highway ride
Was not a clever risk to take
It beat the crowds, it saved us seats
But left us in the freeze
A 45-minute wait outside
Is an unacceptable disease

(Pre-Chorus)
The variable has changed
His health is the priority
The system must be optimized
For sustainability

(Chorus)
The new normal is 6 AM!
A new alarm, a brand new plan
Route 1 Local, a slower pace
A 45-minute ride, a safer space
A 15-minute wait is all we get
A logical and warm asset
This is the system, this is the shield
The old inefficiency must yield!

(Verse 2)
It stops at every local street
It stops at the New River Mall
It's not as fast, it's not "express"
But it's the logical call
We beat the rush, we beat the cold
We're safe inside the library
Before the 7 AM wave
A perfect victory

(Pre-Chorus)
The variable has changed
His health is the priority
The system must be optimized
For sustainability

(Chorus)
The new normal is 6 AM!
A new alarm, a brand new plan
Route 1 Local, a slower pace
A 45-minute ride, a safer space
A 15-minute wait is all we get
A logical and warm asset
This is the system, this is the shield
The old inefficiency must yield!

(Outro)
(Spoken)
"System is stable."
"Commute protocol is active."
"Optimal solution found."

title: The Paper Invasion (CP87A)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Paper Invasion (CP87A)

**LORE NOTE:**
Having lost her battle for legal guardianship, Aubrie Sinclair attempts a new tactic of financial control and harassment by fraudulently claiming Jordan as a dependent on her tax return. This prompts the IRS to issue a Notice CP87A to both of them. When Daniel Brooks hands Jordan the official treasury envelope addressed to his dead name, "Aubrey J. Fuller," the trauma triggers a severe meltdown. However, the panic is short-lived. Anchored by the calm, strategic support of Daniel and Laura Brooks, Jordan channels his fear into resolve. They compile an impenetrable fortress of evidence—including his dorm lease, financial aid records, and bank statements—ready to prove his independence to the federal government, ensuring his mother is picking a fight she is destined to lose.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)

(Verse 1)
(Spoken)
A quiet night in the basement
I'm starting to feel free
Then Daniel holds a letter out
Addressed... to me
Not to Jordan.
No... not to me.

(Pre-Chorus)
An envelope from the Treasury
From the IRS
My legal name, my formal name
A snake in my address
"Aubrey J. Fuller"
My stomach starts to knot
This is her new attack
This is the trap I thought I'd caught

(Chorus)
A PAPER INVASION!
A CEE PEA EIGHTY SEVEN AY!
She's trying to reclaim me
She's trying to make me pay!
She lied upon her taxes
She's fighting with the law
She's trying to prove I'm just a child
A dependent, and a flaw!

(Verse 2)
The letters swim, the numbers blur
It's all I see... just her, just her
The walls are closing in again
This battle never, ever ends
She's in my home, she's in my mail
She's built a new, official jail

(Chorus)
A PAPER INVASION!
A CEE PEA EIGHTY SEVEN AY!
She's trying to reclaim me
She's trying to make me pay!
She lied upon her taxes
She's fighting with the law
She's trying to prove I'm just a child
A dependent, and a flaw!

(Bridge)
(Spoken)
"What happens now?"
"What do we do?"
"She's going to win..."
"She's going to... she's going to..."
NO!

(Outro)
SYSTEM CRASH!
SYSTEM CRASH!

title: I-64 (The Gauntlet)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# I-64 (The Gauntlet)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track explores the visceral, geographic nature of trauma. In an attempt to conquer his demons and see the Blue Ridge Parkway at Rockfish Gap, Jordan agrees to a road trip. However, reaching the gap requires driving on Interstate 64 East. To Jordan, I-64 is not just a highway; it is a psychological "rootkit virus," intrinsically tied to the route back to his abuser, Aubrie Sinclair, in Hampton Roads. The tension mounts during the agonizing 30-mile stretch starting in Lexington, where I-81 and I-64 merge. Despite his desire to be brave, the moment Mark actually takes Exit 221 to continue onto I-64 East in Staunton, Jordan's system catastrophically crashes. The sheer terror of being on the road heading toward his mother induces a severe meltdown, forcing Mark to immediately abort the mission and bail out at the very next ramp—Exit 91 (SR-285 towards Fishersville)—to break the virus's hold.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A fast, pulsing, anxious synth bass, mimicking the hum of tires on asphalt. A high-pitched, digital whine slowly builds in the background—the "rootkit virus" activating.)

(Verse 1)
We're driving up the eighty-one
To see the Parkway in the sun
At Rockfish Gap, a mountain view
A demon that I thought I slew
But thirty miles from Lexington
The double signs begin to run
Sixty-four is on the board
A haunted path, a broken cord

(Pre-Chorus)
(The digital whine gets louder, glitching)
A rootkit virus in my brain
A geographic, phantom pain
It points to the coast, it points to the east
It points to the belly of the beast

(Chorus)
(A driving, heavy, frantic rock beat kicks in)
I-64! The Gauntlet!
The road that goes back to the start!
I-64! The Monster!
It's tearing my logic apart!
Every mile marker, a threat and a snare
I'm trying to fight, but she is still there!
The virus is spreading! I can't break the code!
I'm trapped on this cursed, invisible road!

(Verse 2)
We reach the split, the final test
Exit two-two-one, no time to rest
Mark takes the ramp, the blinker clicks
My lungs are full of burning bricks
The sign says "East," the trap is sprung
The air is stolen from my lungs
I'm heading back! I'm heading down!
To her! To her! To her hometown!

(Pre-Chorus)
A rootkit virus in my brain
A geographic, phantom pain
It points to the coast, it points to the east
It points to the belly of the beast!

(Chorus)
I-64! The Gauntlet!
The road that goes back to the start!
I-64! The Monster!
It's tearing my logic apart!
Every mile marker, a threat and a snare
I'm trying to fight, but she is still there!
The virus is spreading! I can't break the code!
I'm trapped on this cursed, invisible road!

(Bridge)
(The music drops to just the frantic pulse and distorted, panicked vocals)
(Screamed)
NO! TURN AROUND!
I CAN'T DO THIS! I'M GOING DOWN!
THE VIRUS IS ACTIVE! THE SYSTEM IS SHOT!
THIS IS THE ROAD TO THE PLACE I FORGOT!
GET OFF! GET OFF!

(Outro)
(The heavy beat abruptly stops. Only the sound of a car swerving and slowing down remains, fading into a gentle, acoustic strum.)
Mark hits the brakes, he sees the fear
He pulls the wheel, the exit's here
Exit ninety-one...
State route two-eight-five...
Fishersville...
(Heavy, shaking breaths)
I'm still alive.
I'm sorry.
I couldn't do it.

title: 99.8 (The Fever)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# 99.8 (The Fever)

**LORE NOTE:**
This track highlights the lingering, practical consequences of Aubrie Sinclair's medical and emotional neglect. Jordan wakes up feeling unwell and discovers he has a low-grade temperature of 99.8°F. To a typical young adult, this is a minor inconvenience easily solved with over-the-counter medication. But because his mother deliberately weaponized his dependency by never teaching him basic self-care, health literacy, or how to handle minor illnesses, Jordan spirals into a panic. He views this slight fever as a catastrophic system failure. The "very preventable problem" is simply his lack of knowledge on how to take a Tylenol and drink fluids. The track resolves with his chosen family gently stepping in, not just to care for him, but to teach him the basic medical protocols he was maliciously denied.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A woozy, slightly off-kilter synth loop. A rhythmic, sluggish bassline mimicking a slightly elevated, heavy heartbeat.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are groggy, confused, and anxious)
My skin is hot, my joints are tight
I couldn't sleep at all last night
I found the plastic in the drawer
The little screen, the numbered score
It beeps a tone I do not know
A flashing warning, pulsing slow
My system's running hot and strange
A biological, frightening change

(Pre-Chorus)
I don't have the manual, I don't have the code
I'm breaking down on this familiar road
She kept me blind, she kept me weak
I don't know the words to speak...

(Chorus)
(The music swells with a dizzy, anxious energy)
Ninety-nine point eight!
Is it a crisis or a glitch?
I'm staring at the numbers
And my hands begin to twitch
A preventable disaster
A simple, stupid flaw
Because she never taught me
The most basic human law!
I'm burning up in ignorance
A system left to fail
Trapped inside this feverish jail!

(Verse 2)
Do I need a doctor? Do I need to call?
Am I going to crash against the wall?
It's just a little number, but it feels so vast
A phantom from my isolated past
If she were here, she'd take control
And charge a heavy, heavy toll
She'd make me beg for medicine
And let the helplessness sink in

(Pre-Chorus)
I don't have the manual, I don't have the code
I'm breaking down on this familiar road
She kept me blind, she kept me weak
I don't know the words to speak...

(Chorus)
Ninety-nine point eight!
Is it a crisis or a glitch?
I'm staring at the numbers
And my hands begin to twitch
A preventable disaster
A simple, stupid flaw
Because she never taught me
The most basic human law!
I'm burning up in ignorance
A system left to fail
Trapped inside this feverish jail!

(Bridge)
(The dizzy music slows down, stabilizing into a warm, gentle acoustic pattern)
Then Aubrie knocks upon the door
A glass of water, nothing more
Two little pills, a gentle hand
She helps me finally understand
(Spoken, softly)
"It's just a little fever, Jordan."
"You just take these, drink this, and rest."
"You're not breaking down. You just need a patch."

(Outro)
(The music is calm, steady, and soothing)
Ninety-nine point eight...
Just a little glitch...
Two little pills...
A simple, easy switch...
I'm learning how to fix myself.
I'm learning how to heal.
System stabilizing...
Resting...

title: The Leviathan Cut (A Sister's Love)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Leviathan Cut (A Sister's Love)

**LORE NOTE:**
As Jordan recovers from his fever, his chosen family decides to cheer him up with the ultimate movie marathon. Cult-following devotees Jack, Sarah, Molly, and Clare sit him down to watch the legendary, 12-hour, 21-minute "Leviathan Cut" of *The Silver Gauntlet of Aethel*. The film is an absolute 1980s cheesy fantasy adventure bliss. As the movie opens with its epic, synthesizer-and-guitar-driven anthem, "A Sister's Love (Main Theme)," Jordan is instantly captivated. The song, performed by the band Fireflight and featuring the powerful female lead vocals of Alannah Shaw, acts as a musical awakening for him. Watching the protagonist Kaelan embark on a perilous quest to save his twin sister Kaela, Jordan realizes he wants his own life's soundtrack to sound exactly like this: full of vibrant 1980s synthesizer power, driving rock energy, and unapologetic hope.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A dramatic, soaring synthesizer melody builds, joined by a pulsing bassline and a single, echoing electric guitar note, mirroring the movie's main theme)

(Verse 1)
Wrapped in blankets, the fever breaking
The TV glows, a new world making
Jack, Sarah, Molly, and Clare hit play on the screen
A twelve-hour cut, a fantasy dream
The Silver Gauntlet of Aethel begins
A story of magic, of losses and wins

(Pre-Chorus)
Then the music swells, an epic synthesizer sound
A pulsing bass lifts me off the ground
An echoing guitar in a cinematic space
I'm transported to a better place

(Chorus)
A Sister's Love! A burning light!
It's chasing away my darkest night
Alannah Shaw is singing out clear
A 1980s anthem I needed to hear!
The Leviathan Cut, twelve hours long
I'm healing my heart inside of this song!
Give me the synth, give me the power!
I could watch this movie for every hour!

(Verse 2)
I see Kaelan fight for Kaela's life
A brother and sister, overcoming the strife
It's absolute cheesy fantasy bliss
I never knew I was starving for this
The Fireflight track, the power pop beat
It pulls me right up from defeat

(Pre-Chorus)
Then the music swells, an epic synthesizer sound
A pulsing bass lifts me off the ground
An echoing guitar in a cinematic space
I'm transported to a better place

(Chorus)
A Sister's Love! A burning light!
It's chasing away my darkest night
Alannah Shaw is singing out clear
A 1980s anthem I needed to hear!
The Leviathan Cut, twelve hours long
I'm healing my heart inside of this song!
Give me the synth, give me the power!
I could watch this movie for every hour!

(Bridge)
My mother's world was classical and cold
But this is neon, brave, and bold!
No more industrial, heavy dread
I want those 80s synths inside my head!

(Guitar & Synth Solo)

(Chorus)
A Sister's Love! A burning light!
It's chasing away my darkest night
Alannah Shaw is singing out clear
A 1980s anthem I needed to hear!
The Leviathan Cut, twelve hours long
I'm healing my heart inside of this song!
Give me the synth, give me the power!
I could watch this movie for every hour!

(Outro)
The Leviathan Cut...
Give me the 80s...
A Sister's Love...
(The music fades with echoing synthesizers)

title: An 80s Education (The Brooks Mix)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# An 80s Education (The Brooks Mix)

**LORE NOTE:**
After witnessing Jordan's sheer delight at the soundtrack of *The Silver Gauntlet of Aethel*, Daniel Brooks makes it his personal mission to guide "his daughter's boy" through the greatest musical decade. Realizing that Jordan's abusive upbringing denied him the joy of popular culture, Daniel curates the ultimate, definitive mixtape: "The Brooks Mix." This track is a joyous, high-energy explosion of gated snares, analog synthesizers, and stadium rock. It represents Jordan finally shedding the cold, oppressive industrial noise of his past and joyfully embracing the neon, unapologetic optimism of the 1980s. 

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(The loud, mechanical click of a cassette tape sliding into a deck. A brief hiss of tape. Suddenly, a massive, echoing, gated-snare drum fill hits, followed by an incredibly bright, bouncing synth-pop bassline and a shimmering keyboard hook.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are bright, energetic, and smiling)
Daniel comes down to the basement room
Says, "We've got to chase away the gloom"
He's holding a plastic, clear cassette
Says, "Son, you haven't heard the best stuff yet"
"You liked the Gauntlet, you liked the sound?"
"Well, I'm about to spin your head around."
He presses play, the speakers hum
And I feel the beat in my own thumb!

(Pre-Chorus)
The tempo's fast, the chords are bright
A neon explosion in the night
No heavy dread, no classical cage
I'm turning to a brand new page!

(Chorus)
(Massive, layered 80s backing vocals join in)
I'm getting an 80s education!
A synthesizer revelation!
From the power ballads to the stadium rock
We're turning back the digital clock!
The Brooks Mix playing on the stereo
Teaching me everything I need to know
It's loud, it's cheesy, and it sets me free!
This is the soundtrack of the new me!

(Verse 2)
There's a song about a girl in a diamond dress
There's a track about a runaway express
Guitar solos soaring to the sky
Vocals that go impossibly high!
Daniel's playing air guitar by the door
Zoe and Madi are dancing on the floor
I'm tapping my foot, I'm nodding my head
The ghost of the old world is finally dead!

(Pre-Chorus)
The tempo's fast, the chords are bright
A neon explosion in the night
No heavy dread, no classical cage
I'm turning to a brand new page!

(Chorus)
I'm getting an 80s education!
A synthesizer revelation!
From the power ballads to the stadium rock
We're turning back the digital clock!
The Brooks Mix playing on the stereo
Teaching me everything I need to know
It's loud, it's cheesy, and it sets me free!
This is the soundtrack of the new me!

(Bridge)
(The music drops to just a driving bass sequencer and a steady drum machine pulse)
(Spoken, with a laugh)
Track four is six minutes long.
It has a three-minute saxophone solo.
I don't know why... but I love it!
(Sung)
She tried to keep the world so quiet
But now I'm starting a neon riot!

(Epic, screaming, heavily processed 80s electric guitar solo)

(Chorus)
I'm getting an 80s education!
A synthesizer revelation!
From the power ballads to the stadium rock
We're turning back the digital clock!
The Brooks Mix playing on the stereo
Teaching me everything I need to know
It's loud, it's cheesy, and it sets me free!
This is the soundtrack of the new me!

(Outro)
(The synths arpeggiate wildly as the track begins a long, slow 80s fade-out)
The Brooks Mix!
Turn it up!
An 80s education...
(Tape click, stop.)

title: What If I'm Like Her? (Stork & Cradle)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts

title: Logically Unfounded (The Pact of Six)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Logically Unfounded (The Pact of Six)

**LORE NOTE:**
After confronting his deepest fears, Jordan still struggles with a lingering, analytical anxiety planted by his mother: the cynical idea that the unconditional love of his chosen family is "logically unfounded." Why would six people—Aubrie, Madison, Zoe, Lauren, Hannah, and Mark—dedicate themselves to his care, happiness, and protection without any ulterior motive or blood obligation? In response, the group gathers around him to formally declare "The Pact of Six." They dismantle his anxiety by explaining that love isn't a transactional equation to be balanced. Their commitment to him is not a temporary charity project; it is a permanent, foundational truth. The promise they make to him is absolute, undeniable, and crystal clear.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A lone, searching piano melody plays, slightly hesitant. It is suddenly joined by a massive, confident, driving 80s rock drumbeat and a bright, shimmering synthesizer pad.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are inquisitive, searching for an answer)
I run the numbers in my head
I trace the things my mother said
She said I was a heavy weight
A burden left to seal their fate
I look at the variables, I look at the cost
A broken boy who was totally lost
It doesn't compute, it doesn't align
Why would you make my battles thine?

(Pre-Chorus)
My damaged brain is searching for the catch
A hidden fee, a poorly hidden latch
I say to Zoe, "It's logically unfounded."
But then the six of them keep me surrounded.

(Chorus)
(The music explodes into a triumphant, stadium-rock anthem. Multiple voices join together.)
The promise is CRYSTAL CLEAR!
There are no hidden conditions here!
It's not an equation, it's not a transaction
It's a permanent, loving, chemical reaction!
The Pact of Six, a constellation of care
Whenever you fall, we will be there!
You can throw out the math, you can tear up the chart
The only logic we need is the heart!
It's CRYSTAL CLEAR!

(Verse 2)
Aubrie's the anchor, holding the line
Madi's the shield when the shadows combine
Zoe's the compass, routing the way
Lauren's the empathy, brightening the day
Hannah's the laughter, the ultimate hug
Mark is the brother pulling the plug
On all of the darkness, all of the fear
