Six points of light, making it clear!

(Pre-Chorus)
My damaged brain was searching for the catch
A hidden fee, a poorly hidden latch
But looking at them, my doubts are all grounded
Who cares if the love is logically unfounded?!

(Chorus)
The promise is CRYSTAL CLEAR!
There are no hidden conditions here!
It's not an equation, it's not a transaction
It's a permanent, loving, chemical reaction!
The Pact of Six, a constellation of care
Whenever you fall, we will be there!
You can throw out the math, you can tear up the chart
The only logic we need is the heart!
It's CRYSTAL CLEAR!

(Bridge)
(The rock beat drops to a steady, rhythmic clap and a booming bass drum)
(Group Vocals, chanted like a vow)
No blood required! No contracts to sign!
Your safety is ours! Your future is mine!
We chose you! We claim you!
We'll never un-name you!
(Jordan's Vocal, soaring)
The system is stable! The network is whole!
I'm finally healing my fractured soul!

(Guitar & Synth Dual Solo - Bright, face-melting, and joyful)

(Chorus)
The promise is CRYSTAL CLEAR!
There are no hidden conditions here!
It's not an equation, it's not a transaction
It's a permanent, loving, chemical reaction!
The Pact of Six, a constellation of care
Whenever you fall, we will be there!
You can throw out the math, you can tear up the chart
The only logic we need is the heart!
It's CRYSTAL CLEAR!

(Outro)
(The music rides out on a wave of triumphant synthesizers)
The Pact of Six...
Crystal clear!
Crystal clear!
Logically unfounded... and absolutely true.

title: Friday, 9:32 AM (The Attack)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Friday, 9:32 AM (The Attack)

**LORE NOTE:**
Just as Jordan finally begins to accept the unconditional love of his chosen family, the years of extreme psychological terror, chronic stress, and profound medical neglect inflicted by Aubrie Sinclair culminate in a sudden, catastrophic physical failure. Despite being only nineteen years old, Jordan suffers a critical STEMI (ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction)—a massive, full-blown heart attack. The event strikes out of nowhere on a routine Friday morning at exactly 9:32 AM. Musically, the album violently shifts gears: the bright, triumphant 80s pop-rock is abruptly severed, replaced by a chaotic, racing, irregular tempo that mirrors his failing heart. It is a terrifying reminder that while his mind is finally safe, his body still bears the lethal, physical toll of his abuser's reign. 

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(The bright 80s synth from the previous track suddenly cuts out with the harsh sound of a needle scratching a record. A moment of dead silence. Then, a high-pitched, ringing tinnitus tone. A bass drum kicks in—erratic, panicked, and painfully heavy, mimicking an irregular, failing heartbeat.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are breathless, strained, and terrified)
It's Friday morning, the sun is bright
We're laughing in the kitchen light
A normal day, a normal plan
I'm standing up... a normal man
But then a shadow hits my chest
A crushing weight, an anvil press
The room is spinning, tilting down
I cannot hear a single sound

(Pre-Chorus)
(The rhythm becomes frantic, anxious)
My left arm goes entirely numb
The air is gone, my throat is dumb
I drop the glass, it hits the floor
I'm falling back against the door
I'm nineteen years old! This cannot be!
What is my body doing to me?!

(Chorus)
(Music is a chaotic, heavy, driving assault)
NINE THIRTY-TWO AM!
The system fails! The final blow!
A critical STEMI taking hold!
A heart attack at nineteen years old!
The stress, the trauma, the years of fear
Have finally come to kill me here!
The damage done, the toll it takes
The moment that the engine breaks!

(Verse 2)
(Vocals are fragmented, drifting in and out of focus)
Aubrie's screaming, she hits the ground
She's holding my head, spinning around
"Call 9-1-1! Call them right now!"
Madi is crying, wiping my brow
The pain is a fire, a searing white
I'm losing my grip, I'm losing the fight
The ghost of my mother, the stress that she built
Is drowning my heart in a river of guilt

(Pre-Chorus)
I'm nineteen years old! I just got free!
Why is this happening to me?!
I finally found a place to belong
And now my heart is beating wrong!

(Chorus)
NINE THIRTY-TWO AM!
The system fails! The final blow!
A critical STEMI taking hold!
A heart attack at nineteen years old!
The stress, the trauma, the years of fear
Have finally come to kill me here!
The damage done, the toll it takes
The moment that the engine breaks!

(Bridge)
(The heavy music drops away. Just the erratic bass drum, fading slowly. The vocals are a weak whisper.)
I don't want to go...
I just got here...
Zoe, I'm scared...
Aubrie, please don't let me go...
It hurts so much...
(A faint sound of sirens in the far distance)

(Guitar Solo - Frantic, screaming, and dissonant, imitating the sirens getting closer)

(Outro)
(The erratic heartbeat drum slows down dangerously)
Nine thirty-two...
System failing...
(Spoken, distant voices)
"Stay with us, Jordan! Stay with us!"
"Ambulance is pulling up!"
"He's crashing!"
(A flatline synthesizer tone holds, then abruptly cuts to silence.)

title: The Longest Night (NPO)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Longest Night (NPO)

**LORE NOTE:**
Jordan survives the catastrophic STEMI, but the immediate aftermath introduces him to the grueling, unglamorous reality of inpatient cardiac recovery. This track captures his first night in the hospital. He is placed on strict "NPO" (nil per os / nothing by mouth) status in case he needs emergency surgery, leaving him intensely, frustratingly hungry and thirsty. To compound his misery, the nurses wake him every two hours for mandatory vitals checks, wrapping a cold blood pressure cuff around his arm just as he drifts off. Yet, amidst the sterile environment, the incessant beeping, and the gnawing hunger, there is a profound comfort: Aubrie Kelly refuses to leave his side. Sleeping in a terribly uncomfortable vinyl hospital chair, her presence transforms a night of physical misery into a quiet testament of enduring, unconditional love.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A slow, steady, rhythmic electronic "beep... beep..." mimicking a stable heart monitor. A soft, melodic acoustic guitar enters, gentle but weary.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are tired, raspy, and slightly annoyed)
The blood pressure cuff inflates again
It's two AM, or maybe ten?
The nurse comes in with a flashlight beam
And rips me out of a fever dream
A poke, a prod, a gentle sigh
"Just checking your vitals, the numbers are high."
She leaves the room, the door clicks shut
And my stomach churns in a hungry rut

(Pre-Chorus)
NPO. Nothing by mouth.
My whole digestive tract has gone south.
I would trade my soul for a single chip
For a drop of water upon my lip
I'm starving, I'm tired, the wires are tight
Welcome to the longest night

(Chorus)
(Music swells slightly, adding a warm cello)
This is the longest night I've ever known
A sterile room in a clinical zone
Beeping machines and a flashing light
No sleep to be found in the longest night
But then I look to my left, and the panic clears
Because the best thing in my life is sitting right here

(Verse 2)
In a vinyl chair that was built for pain
Underneath the glow of the window pane
Aubrie is sleeping, her head rests low
She refused to leave, she refused to go
My mother would have left me to suffer alone
Or complained about the time on her cellular phone
But Aubrie's just breathing, holding my hand
The fiercest protector in all of the land

(Pre-Chorus)
NPO. Nothing by mouth.
My whole digestive tract has gone south.
I'm dreaming of a burger and a massive shake
But I'll bear this hunger for both our sakes
I'm starving, I'm tired, the wires are tight
Welcome to the longest night

(Chorus)
This is the longest night I've ever known
A sterile room in a clinical zone
Beeping machines and a flashing light
No sleep to be found in the longest night
But then I look to my left, and the panic clears
Because the best thing in my life is sitting right here

(Bridge)
(Just the acoustic guitar and the steady heart monitor beep)
(Spoken, with a tired chuckle)
Four AM. The cuff squeezes again.
I ask the nurse if I can just have one ice chip.
She says no.
I look at Aubrie. She opens one eye, smiles, and squeezes my hand.
I guess I can wait until morning.

(Guitar Solo - Slow, bluesy, and full of longing, echoing the feeling of an empty stomach and a full heart)

(Chorus)
(Music is full, warm, and acoustic)
This is the longest night I've ever known
A sterile room in a clinical zone
Beeping machines and a flashing light
No sleep to be found in the longest night
But I'm not alone, and the panic clears
Because the best thing in my life is sitting right here

(Outro)
(The acoustic guitar fades slowly)
Just one cracker...
Please...
(The monitor beeps steadily)
I love you, Aubrie.
(Deep breath, fading to silence)

title: A CVICU Romance (Intimacy v2)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# A CVICU Romance (Intimacy v2)

**LORE NOTE:**
Following his massive heart attack, Jordan is transferred to Star City General Hospital in Roanoke, where he undergoes a major CABG (Coronary Artery Bypass Graft) surgery. To ensure he is never alone in the Cardiovascular Intensive Care Unit (CVICU), the single women of his pact institute a strict, rotating shift schedule. It is currently Zoe's turn when a severe blizzard strikes the Roanoke Valley, completely snowing her in. Zoe, who is also autistic, processes the world through a deeply logical, computational framework. For her, romance isn't about grand poetic gestures; it is about analyzing data, optimizing his comfort, and monitoring his telemetry. As the snow howls outside the sterile hospital window, Zoe redefines affection for Jordan. She demonstrates "Intimacy v2"—a love language built on unwavering stability, perfectly calculated care, and the beautiful logic of a system functioning exactly as it was programmed to: keeping him safe.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A soft, atmospheric synthesizer hums like a quiet hospital room. A steady, rhythmic, electronic pulse begins—a healthy, stable heart monitor. Gentle, icy synth notes mimic the snow falling outside.)

(Verse 1)
(Male vocals are weak but peaceful; Female vocals are clear, precise, and calm)
(Male Vocal)
The Roanoke wind is howling at the glass
The weather radar says this storm won't pass
My chest is wired, the sternum stitched and sore
A bypass graft, a quiet, bloody war
But I'm not scared, the panic doesn't rise
Because I see the data in your eyes

(Female Vocal - Zoe)
The rotation schedule has been compromised.
The external variables are unadvised.
A blizzard warning traps me in this space.
But my designated coordinate is this exact place.
I'm reading your telemetry, the rhythm is secure.
The input is stable, the output is pure.

(Pre-Chorus)
(The synth pad warms up, feeling less sterile and more comforting)
(Male Vocal)
She doesn't hold my hand and softly weep
She analyzes the angle of my sleep
(Female Vocal)
I'm optimizing the blankets for your thermal state.
I'm monitoring the fluid at a steady rate.

(Chorus)
(A gentle, driving synth-pop beat drops in—warm, analog, and steady)
(Both)
This is a CVICU Romance!
(Female Vocal)
No unpredictable, chaotic dance!
(Male Vocal)
It's Intimacy Version Two!
A beautiful algorithm, shining through!
(Female Vocal)
Love is a protocol that will not fail.
A logical system to tip the scale.
I've calculated the risk, I've run the test.
My optimal function is guarding your rest.

(Verse 2)
(Female Vocal)
Your mother's logic was a virus in your mind.
A destructive loop, maliciously designed.
But I operate on a different set of code.
I am here to carry the excess load.
Your heart was damaged, the tubing was blocked.
But the surgical parameters are firmly locked.

(Male Vocal)
She watches the monitor above my head
More closely than the words I might have said
To her, my steady pulse is poetry
The finest romantic song there could ever be

(Pre-Chorus)
(Male Vocal)
She doesn't hold my hand and softly weep
She analyzes the angle of my sleep
(Female Vocal)
I'm optimizing the blankets for your thermal state.
I'm monitoring the fluid at a steady rate.

(Chorus)
(Both)
This is a CVICU Romance!
(Female Vocal)
No unpredictable, chaotic dance!
(Male Vocal)
It's Intimacy Version Two!
A beautiful algorithm, shining through!
(Female Vocal)
Love is a protocol that will not fail.
A logical system to tip the scale.
I've calculated the risk, I've run the test.
My optimal function is guarding your rest.

(Bridge)
(The beat strips away. Just the icy synth and the steady heartbeat monitor)
(Male Vocal)
They say that logic is a cold and empty thing
But they haven't heard the way her systems sing
She computes my needs before I have to ask
She treats my healing as her primary task
(Spoken - Zoe)
"Jordan. Your heart rate is elevated by three beats per minute."
"Are you experiencing pain, or is it an emotional response?"
(Spoken - Jordan)
"It's an emotional response, Zoe. I just really love you."
(Spoken - Zoe)
"Understood. The feeling is completely mutual. I will log this data."

(Gentle, melodic synth solo - logical, yet incredibly warm)

(Chorus)
(Both)
This is a CVICU Romance!
(Female Vocal)
No unpredictable, chaotic dance!
(Male Vocal)
It's Intimacy Version Two!
A beautiful algorithm, shining through!
(Female Vocal)
Love is a protocol that will not fail.
A logical system to tip the scale.
I've calculated the risk, I've run the test.
My optimal function is guarding your rest.

(Outro)
(The music fades, leaving only the steady beep of the heart monitor)
(Female Vocal)
System optimal.
Vitals stable.
Commencing sleep cycle.
(Male Vocal)
Goodnight, Zoe.

title: Cardiac Rehab (The Breaking Point)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# Cardiac Rehab (The Breaking Point)

**LORE NOTE:**
Surviving the heart attack and the CABG surgery was only the beginning; now Jordan must face the grueling, agonizing marathon of Cardiac Rehab. At just nineteen years old, he finds himself on treadmills and lifting light weights alongside patients four times his age, battling the terrifying fragility of his healing sternum and the profound exhaustion of his traumatized body. This track captures the physical and psychological "breaking point" where the pain and frustration make him want to quit entirely. However, unlike his isolated past, he is surrounded by the unbreakable wall of his chosen family. Mark acts as his spotter, Hannah brings him water, and the entire Pact of Six anchors him. When his body screams to give up, their collective strength carries him across the finish line, proving that recovery is not a solo endeavor.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A slow, heavy, repetitive drumbeat, mimicking the exhausted, dragging footsteps on a treadmill. A low, grinding bassline builds tension, accompanied by the hiss of an oxygen cannula.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are strained, breathless, and full of exertion)
Fluorescent lights and a rubber floor
Every step is a heavy, brutal war
The wires pull beneath my shirt
Every single breath is designed to hurt
I'm nineteen years old, I shouldn't be here
Walking on a track fueled by clinical fear
The sternum aches, the muscles scream
Trapped inside this slow-motion dream

(Pre-Chorus)
The incline rises, the resistance grows
How much further? Nobody knows.
The monitor flashes, the numbers climb
I'm a prisoner of this healing time
I want to rip off the leads, I want to fall
I am staring at the impossible wall!

(Chorus)
(The music hits hard, a heavy, driving, mid-tempo rock anthem)
This is the breaking point! The edge of the fray!
The moment my body just wants to give way!
The engine is stalling, the spirit is cracked
A nineteen-year-old on his back, under attack!
But I look to my left, and I look to my right
I'm not doing this on my own tonight!
They're holding the line, they're taking the weight
We're pushing right through the breaking point gate!

(Verse 2)
Mark is beside me, matching my pace
A brotherly sweat on his focused face
Hannah is waiting at the end of the line
With a bottle of water and a "you're doing fine"
Aubrie and Madi, Zoe, Lauren, too
A gallery of love that is pulling me through
When my knees start to buckle, when my vision goes gray
They are the anchor that keeps me in play

(Pre-Chorus)
The incline rises, the resistance grows
How much further? Nobody knows.
But I hear their voices, a chorus so loud
A beautiful, stubborn, and furious crowd!
I won't rip off the leads! I refuse to fall!
We are breaking right through the impossible wall!

(Chorus)
This is the breaking point! The edge of the fray!
The moment my body just wants to give way!
The engine is stalling, the spirit is cracked
A nineteen-year-old on his back, under attack!
But I look to my left, and I look to my right
I'm not doing this on my own tonight!
They're holding the line, they're taking the weight
We're pushing right through the breaking point gate!

(Bridge)
(The heavy beat drops to just a pulsing bass and a snare drum snapping like a whip)
(Spoken, breathing heavily)
"I can't... I can't do another mile."
(Spoken - Mark)
"Yes you can, buddy. Just look at me. One step."
(Spoken - Aubrie)
"We're right here, Jordan. We've got you."
(Sung)
The trauma told me I was weak and alone
But this family is carved out of iron and bone!
One more step! One more breath!
We're walking away from the shadow of death!

(Guitar Solo - Sweaty, triumphant, and soaring, fighting against the heavy rhythm)

(Chorus)
This is the breaking point! The edge of the fray!
The moment my body just wants to give way!
The engine is stalling, the spirit is cracked
A nineteen-year-old on his back, under attack!
But I look to my left, and I look to my right
I'm not doing this on my own tonight!
They're holding the line, they're taking the weight
We're pushing right through the breaking point gate!

(Outro)
(The heavy drumbeat gradually slows down, stabilizing into a normal, resting tempo)
Pushing through...
We made it through...
(A deep, clear exhale)
System stabilizing.
Rehab complete.

title: The Accessibility Project
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Accessibility Project

**LORE NOTE:**
Now firmly in his recovery phase and back to his coursework, Jordan and his chosen family tackle a massive class assignment: mapping CPI's accessibility features. The group realistically acknowledges that they cannot physically fix the campus—they don't have the budget to pour concrete ramps or install new elevators. However, they realize that *information* is just as powerful as infrastructure. They set out to create comprehensive maps detailing every automatic door, hidden ramp, and accessible path across the rugged terrain. A major victory in their research is mapping the "Hillside Pass-Throughs." They document the university's policy allowing disabled non-residents to get their student IDs specially encoded by the Office of Educational Accessibility. This grants 24/7 access to the 1st and 2nd-floor lobbies and the elevator of buildings built on inclines—like Jordan's old dorm, "The Old Lady on the Hill" (Eleanor Bennett Hall)—allowing students to safely bypass steep hills regardless of standard guest hours. Musically, this track is an upbeat, focused, and triumphant "heist" song, turning data collection into a grand adventure.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A fast, driving, rhythmic synth pulse. The sound of typing on a keyboard, mouse clicks, and a crisp, energetic bassline. It feels like a high-tech mission briefing.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are fast, rhythmic, and collaborative, bouncing between group members)
(Jordan) We got the syllabus, we read the brief
(Zoe) We need a system to provide relief
(Mark) We cannot pour the concrete for a ramp
(Madi) We cannot buy an elevator for the camp
(Jordan) The budget's zero, but the mission's clear
We have to help the people navigate the fear
(Aubrie) If we can't break the bricks and tear the walls
(All) We'll map the secrets hidden in the halls!

(Pre-Chorus)
(The beat intensifies, adding driving rock drums)
Surveying the campus, plotting the lines!
Finding the routes behind the broken designs!
The automatic doors, the hidden terrain
We're charting a path right through the pain!

(Chorus)
(A bright, massive, soaring 80s pop-rock chorus)
The Accessibility Project!
We're building a map, we're sharing the key!
The Accessibility Project!
For everyone else who is fighting to see!
We can't fix the stairs, we can't level the ground
But we'll show you the way that we safely get around!
Drawing the grid, unlocking the door
We're leveling the playing field, settling the score!

(Verse 2)
(Jordan) Let's take The Old Lady on the Hill
Eleanor Bennett and her steep incline chill
(Mark) Guest hours lock it down at midnight flat
If you're not a resident, you're stuck with that
(Zoe) But wait, I found a protocol hidden in the code!
An accommodation to lighten the load!
Get your ID encoded by the O-E-A
And you can use the elevator any time of day!

(Pre-Chorus)
First floor to the second, an authorized ride!
A Hillside Pass-Through, unlocking the inside!
No residential hallways, just bypassing the steep
A 24-hour promise the campus has to keep!

(Chorus)
The Accessibility Project!
We're building a map, we're sharing the key!
The Accessibility Project!
For everyone else who is fighting to see!
We can't fix the stairs, we can't level the ground
But we'll show you the way that we safely get around!
Drawing the grid, unlocking the door
We're leveling the playing field, settling the score!

(Bridge)
(The music drops to a quiet, pulsing sequencer. Jordan's vocal is earnest and clear)
I remember the panic, I remember the cold
The feeling of being shut out and controlled
So this isn't a project, and this isn't a grade
This is the foundation of a brand new crusade
For the kid in a chair, for the girl with a cane
We are removing the physical strain
Knowledge is power! The data is free!

(Guitar & Synth Solo - Fast, technical, and triumphant)

(Chorus)
(All voices together, maximum volume)
The Accessibility Project!
We're building a map, we're sharing the key!
The Accessibility Project!
For everyone else who is fighting to see!
We can't fix the stairs, we can't level the ground
But we'll show you the way that we safely get around!
Drawing the grid, unlocking the door
We're leveling the playing field, settling the score!

(Outro)
(The synth pulse slowly fades out with the sound of a printer printing a map)
Map complete.
Paths are clear.
The Old Lady's unlocked.
Project uploaded.

title: The Eleventh Child (A Gauntlet of Hearts)
album: The Eleventh Child: A Gauntlet of Hearts
content:
# The Eleventh Child (A Gauntlet of Hearts)

**LORE NOTE:**
This is the grand finale. Jordan has survived the absolute worst his abuser, his own failing body, and the world could throw at him. He has crossed his own personal "Silver Gauntlet." The title, "The Eleventh Child," is a poetic metaphor to Jordan; he feels as though he was reborn and saved at the "eleventh hour," adopted into a massive, sprawling network of love as a new child of the light. (The universe holds a deeper, biological irony to this title, but to Jordan, it simply means miraculous salvation). The "Gauntlet of Hearts" refers to the unbreakable wall formed by his chosen family—the Pact of Six, the Brooks, and everyone who stood by him. Musically, this track is the ultimate 1980s stadium-rock euphoric anthem. It is loud, unapologetically joyful, and exploding with triumphant synthesizers and soaring guitars. He is 100% safe, 100% secure, and entirely free.

**LYRICS:**

(Intro)
(A massive, sweeping, cinematic synthesizer chord rings out, bright and golden. A thundering, stadium-sized drum fill echoes through the space. The lead guitar kicks in with a blazing, triumphant, major-key riff. Pure 80s euphoria.)

(Verse 1)
(Vocals are incredibly strong, confident, and full of joy)
I started in the shadows, in an industrial cage
I was written in the margins of a forgotten page
I walked through the fire, I survived the bitter cold
I beat a failing heart at nineteen years old!
She tried to keep me captive, she tried to drag me down
But I built a brand new kingdom in a brand new town!

(Pre-Chorus)
They tried to cut the wire, they tried to break the line
But the system is immortal, and the victory is mine!
The past is just a ghost that I've learned how to erase
I've found my optimal coordinate, I've found my perfect space!

(Chorus)
(The music explodes. Multiple voices join in a massive choir of chosen family)
I was pulled from the dark at the eleventh hour!
A brand new child of the synthesizer power!
I walked through the fire, I played my part
And now I'm surrounded by a GAUNTLET OF HEARTS!
The tyrant is vanquished, the shadows are clear
I am finally, totally, permanently here!
I'm the Eleventh Child! And I am free!
Safe inside my chosen family!

(Verse 2)
The Pact of Six is standing, a fortress made of light
We conquered the highway, we won the longest night
No more rootkit viruses, no more endless fear
We washed away the trauma in a flood of happy tears!
Zoe's got the logic, Madi's got the shield
Aubrie is the anchor on this battlefield!
Daniel's playing mixtapes, Laura leaves the light on
We're staring at the dawn until the night is gone!

(Pre-Chorus)
They tried to cut the wire, they tried to break the line
But the system is immortal, and the victory is mine!
The past is just a ghost that I've learned how to erase
I've found my optimal coordinate, I've found my perfect space!

(Chorus)
I was pulled from the dark at the eleventh hour!
A brand new child of the synthesizer power!
I walked through the fire, I played my part
And now I'm surrounded by a GAUNTLET OF HEARTS!
The tyrant is vanquished, the shadows are clear
I am finally, totally, permanently here!
I'm the Eleventh Child! And I am free!
Safe inside my chosen family!

(Bridge)
(The heavy drums drop out. A shimmering, arpeggiated synth plays underneath a soaring, melodic bassline. It feels like flying.)
Just like Kaelan on the silver movie screen
I am living out the most impossible dream
The twin suns are rising, the gold and the silver bright
I have officially defeated the night!
(The drums build back up, a rapid-fire snare roll leading to the climax)

(Guitar & Synth Dual Solo)
(A face-melting, joyful, technically brilliant solo where the analog synthesizer and the electric guitar trade melodies, perfectly complementing each other)

(Chorus)
(Maximum volume, maximum joy. The entire cast is singing.)
I was pulled from the dark at the eleventh hour!
A brand new child of the synthesizer power!
I walked through the fire, I played my part
And now I'm surrounded by a GAUNTLET OF HEARTS!
The tyrant is vanquished, the shadows are clear
I am finally, totally, permanently here!
I'm the Eleventh Child! And I am free!
Safe inside my chosen family!

(Outro)
(The music rides the euphoric wave, the synth melody repeating as it begins a long fade)
A Gauntlet of Hearts!
(Spoken - Jordan)
"System is perfect."
We won!
The Eleventh Child...
Safe.
(The final chord holds out, shimmering and warm, until it fades completely into a peaceful silence.)

title: Electric Color
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The solid **#2 hit**. It sat at the runner-up spot for six weeks, famously blocked from #1 by a certain "novelty song" that swept the summer of '87.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Woke up this morning, the world was gray
Didn't know the colors had all faded away
Been living in a room with the curtains drawn tight
Then you flipped a switch and turned on the light

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Verse 2)
The city sounds were just a buzz in my ear
A static station I could never quite hear
But now the noise is turning into a song
And I finally feel like I belong

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Bridge)
The beat is getting stronger
I can't hide it any longer
My heart is playing catch-up
To the music turning way up!

(Guitar Solo)

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Outro)
Electric color
Feeling so new
It's all because of you

title: Plastic Sky
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's first act of "malicious compliance." He hid angsty, anti-corporate lyrics about "living under a plastic sky" inside a polished, studio-approved pop-rock wrapper. It is a direct protest against the "thin" sound the studio demanded—Ryan sings explicitly about his guitar being "clean" and the drums being "thin" while the executives nodded along, missing the insult completely.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The camera's on, the smile is bright
Another take in the burning light
They painted the set with a perfect blue
But it doesn't feel real, it doesn't feel true

(Chorus)
We're living under a plastic sky (Oh-oh-oh)
They tell me "jump," I ask "how high?"
(Oh-oh-oh)
It's just a set, it's all a lie
Living under a plastic sky

(Verse 2)
My guitar is clean, the drums are thin
They polished away all the grit and sin
I want to scream, I want to roar
But they just ask me to sing some more

(Chorus)
We're living under a plastic sky (Oh-oh-oh)
They tell me "jump," I ask "how high?"
(Oh-oh-oh)
It's just a set, it's all a lie
Living under a plastic sky

(Bridge)
Is this the dream we were chasing?
A different part, a different face
I'm losing me, I'm losing you
In all this shiny, plastic blue

(Guitar Solo)

(Chorus)
We're living under a plastic sky (Oh-oh-oh)
They tell me "jump," I ask "how high?"
(Oh-oh-oh)
It's just a set, it's all a lie
Living under a plastic sky

(Outro)
Plastic sky...
Feeling so new...
(fades out)

title: My Anchor Holds
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
A power ballad the studio marketed as a romance, but which the band wrote about their family bond. This "sibling-love-disguised-as-romance" became a staple of their early sound.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The lights are bright, but the room is cold
Another story that's bought and sold
I'm trying to find a reason why
Beneath this plastic, empty sky

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Verse 2)
They pull us under, they push us high
They build us up just to watch us die
It's hard to know who you can trust
When all the promises turn to dust

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Guitar Solo)

(Bridge)
So let them talk, let them stare
As long as you are standing there
I'm not afraid of what's to come
I know where I belong...

(Chorus)
'Cause when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Outro)
My anchor...
My anchor holds...
(Fades out with piano and synth)

title: Dancing in the Neon
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
"I wanna see you dancing to this one, Chicago!" A crowd-pleaser that fit the label's "pop" image perfectly. This was one of the "safe" synth-pop singles Apex Records pushed for, prioritizing high energy and polished production over the band's rock roots.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The city's quiet, the sun is down
We're in the glow of the other side of town
The beat is calling from a place I know
Just follow the sound, and let it go

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Verse 2)
The rhythm's heavy, a flashing bright
Reflected colors in your eyes
I don't care what the real world wants
This moment's all that I could ever want

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Bridge)
Don't stop, don't stop the feeling
This music's got me reeling
Don't stop, don't stop the beat
(Synth-pop breakdown / keyboard solo)

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Outro)
Dancing in the neon... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
'Til the night is gone...
(fades)

title: Just a Girl
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Cassidy's quiet rebellion. Apex executives dismissed this moody, introspective track as "filler," but it became a grassroots anthem for female fans. The honest lyrics about her treatment in the industry ("They tell me 'sing the high note' / They tell me 'wear the red dress'") resonated deeply with young women who felt similarly boxed in by expectations.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
They tell me "sing the high note"
They tell me "wear the red dress"
They never ask me what I wrote
They only want to see the press

(Verse 2)
I bring my chords, my melody
They pat my head and disagree
They turn to him, "now *that's* a sound"
And I'm just standing in the background

(Chorus)
Am I ever gonna break through?
Am I ever gonna get my due?
Or am I just a girl in the band?
A pretty picture, holding your hand

(Verse 3)
They say "be 'Stardust', be the grace"
"Know your role and keep your place"
But I am more than what you see
I'm fighting just to be... me

(Chorus)
Am I ever gonna break through?
Am I ever gonna get my due?
Or am I just a girl in the band?
A pretty picture, holding your hand

(Bridge)
But the music's in my blood
The synth, the key, the flood
You can't erase the fire
My voice is getting higher

(Chorus)
Am I ever gonna break through?
Am I ever gonna get my due?
Or am I just a girl in the band?
A pretty picture, holding your hand

(Outro)
Just a girl...
Just a girl...
(fades out on a shimmering synth pad)

title: Light Up the Night
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The song Apex Records poured *all* of their money into. They were convinced this polished synth-pop track would be "The Big One." It wasn't. Despite the massive marketing campaign, the "safe" sound failed to connect with audiences in the way the band's authentic rock tracks did.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
I was walking in the shadows
I was living in the gray
Waiting for a signal
To help me find my way

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Verse 2)
This city sleeps, but we're awake
A promise that we'll never break
The future's ours, I see the sign
Just put your hand in mine

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Bridge)
No more shadows, no more gray
(Keyboard Solo)

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Outro)
Light up the night... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Shining so bright...
(fades)

title: Break the Walls
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The one "pure rock" anthem Apex allowed on the album. It opened Side B and became an immediate fan-favorite for its raw energy. While the studio pushed "Light Up the Night," the fans embraced this track as the true spirit of the band.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Been counting the days in this neon cage
Smiling for the cameras on a plastic stage
They turned the synths up, drowned the sound
But you can't keep a good thing down

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Verse 2)
I'm tired of the glitter, tired of the gloss
Tired of the suits who think they're the boss
This guitar is loaded, it's ready to scream
Waking up from their pretty dream

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Guitar Solo)
(A blistering, technical, shredding solo that Ryan fought to keep on the record)

(Bridge)
For every kid who feels the same
Trapped in a world, playing their game
This one's for you, this one's for me
Time to be free!

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Outro)
Let it out!
Break the walls!
C'mon!
(fades on a hanging guitar chord)

title: Worlds Apart
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
An epic mid-tempo rock ballad that defied the studio's attempt to separate the band's "Stardust" and "Engine" personas. It features a massive, soaring duet between Ryan and Cassidy, proving their voices worked best when fighting together rather than apart. The lyrics in the third verse ("They want the 'Stardust,' they want the 'Engine'") are a direct meta-commentary on their struggle with Apex Records.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Another mile on a lonely road
Just trying to get back to you
I'm carrying this heavy load
They're telling me what I have to do

(Verse 2)
The lights are bright, the stage is cold
They're trying to fit me in their mold
I see your face across the black
I know that we can find our way back

(Chorus)
We're worlds apart, but our hearts beat the same (Oh-oh)
I can hear you call my name
Through the distance, through the static and the dark
We're finding a way back to the start (Oh-oh)
Even when we're worlds apart

(Verse 3)
They want the "Stardust," they want the "Engine"
A different version for a different show
But we're the ones who'll be here in the end
We've got a fire they'll never know

(Chorus)
We're worlds apart, but our hearts beat the same (Oh-oh)
I can hear you call my name
Through the distance, through the static and the dark
We're finding a way back to the start (Oh-oh)
Even when we're worlds apart

(Guitar Solo)

(Bridge)
And when the mountains fall
And when the oceans rise
I'll find you (I'll find you!)

(Chorus)
We're worlds apart, but our hearts beat the same (Oh-oh)
I can hear you call my name
Through the distance, through the static and the dark
We're finding a way back to the start (Oh-oh)
Even when we're worlds apart

(Outro)
Worlds apart...
(fades)

title: Another Night
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
A "safe" filler track demanded by Apex to pad out the album with radio-friendly pop. Ryan was explicitly banned from playing on this track ("No guitar allowed" was written on the session sheet), leaving Cassidy isolated with just a synthesizer backing track. Her vocal performance is genuinely upset, but not because of a breakup—she was miserable recording without her brother in the studio.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The clock on the wall is ticking slow
I'm watching the rain fall on the window
I pick up the phone, but I let it go
Just waiting for a voice I know

(Chorus)
It's just another night without you (Oh-oh)
Another night I've got to get through
The city's sleeping, but I'm awake
Another night, for goodness sake

(Verse 2)
The TV's on, but the sound is low
I've got no other place to go
The silence is a heavy sound
I wish that you were here right now

(Chorus)
It's just another night without you (Oh-oh)
Another night I've got to get through
The city's sleeping, but I'm awake
Another night, for goodness sake

(Bridge)
(A very simple, minimal, clean keyboard solo. No guitar allowed.)

(Chorus)
It's just another night without you (Oh-oh)
Another night I've got to get through
The city's sleeping, but I'm awake
Another night, for goodness sake

(Outro)
Another night...
Another night...
(fades out)

title: Out of Time
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's punk-inspired vent. The 13-track album was too long for a standard vinyl LP, so Apex executives "gleefully" cut this short, 1:35 track from the original vinyl release to save space. They viewed it as "noise," but to Ryan, cutting his rawest song was a direct attempt to silence the "Engine."

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Tick-tock goes the plastic clock
Another meeting on another block
They're selling me a future that I don't want
It's just the same old tired font

(Chorus)
I'm out of patience!
I'm out of time!
This ain't your product, this life is mine!
Out of patience!
Out of time!
Gonna break free, gonna cross the line!

(Verse 2)
You want another ballad, soft and slow
Another song for the radio
But I've got a fire burning in my hands
You'll never, ever understand

(Chorus)
I'm out of patience!
I'm out of time!
This ain't your product, this life is mine!
Out of patience!
Out of time!
Gonna break free, gonna cross the line!

(Guitar Solo)
(Short, fast, and chaotic. Pure punk-rock energy)

(Chorus)
I'm out of patience!
I'm out of time!
This ain't your product, this life is mine!
Out of patience!
Out of time!
Gonna break free, gonna cross the line!

(Outro)
Out of time!
(Song ends abruptly on a final, crashing chord)

title: Rearview Mirror
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
A moody, atmospheric "driving song" that sets the stage for the album's finale. While Apex approved it for its polished synth-pop sound, Cassidy used the lyrics to express her feeling of being a "passenger" in her own career. The poignant line "With all this 'Stardust' left inside" serves as a double entendre for her suppressed creative potential vs. the manufactured image she was forced to project.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Another city, another night
We're speeding past the exit light
I see the skyline start to fade
A memory of a choice I made

(Chorus)
I'm looking in the rearview mirror
The past is getting clearer
Was it all just a mistake?
A heart that's learning how to break
It's all in the rearview mirror

(Verse 2)
This neon glow feels cold and thin
I don't know where I'm supposed to begin
I'm just a passenger in this ride
With all this "Stardust" left inside

(Chorus)
I'm looking in the rearview mirror
The past is getting clearer
Was it all just a mistake?
A heart that's learning how to break
It's all in the rearview mirror

(Bridge)
(A moody, atmospheric keyboard solo)
I'm driving fast, but I'm not free
Just running from the girl I used to be

(Chorus)
I'm looking in the rearview mirror
The past is getting clearer
Was it all just a mistake?
A heart that's learning how to break
It's all in the rearview mirror

(Outro)
Fading... fading...
In the rearview mirror...
(fades)

title: The Stardust Engine
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Buried at the end of the album, this is the band's true anthem. It is the only track on the debut that perfectly blends the "Stardust" (synths) and "Engine" (rock) identities. Critics and chart historians agree: had Apex released this as the lead single instead of burying it, it would have spent **11 straight weeks at #1**, dethroning the existing record holder for the decade.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
We were looking for a signal
A light in the endless black
A single voice in the universe
To tell us we're on the right track

(Verse 2)
We found the fire in the wires
We found the heart in the machine
Building a dream from nothing
A sound the world has never seen

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Verse 3)
They tried to ground us, keep us small
Tell us that we were gonna fall
But they don't know the things we know
They haven't seen the places we'll go

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Guitar Solo)
(A soaring, melodic solo that trades licks with a bright synthesizer hook)

(Bridge)
We are the cosmos, we are the fire
We're taking this one, higher and higher
We are the Stardust!
(We are the Engine!)
We are the Stardust Engine!

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Outro)
Shining so bright...
We're taking flight...
(fades out on an epic, sustained guitar chord)

title: Neon Hearts
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The "safe" title track. Apex forced the band to include a callback lyric to their first hit ("It's an electric-colored feeling") to ensure brand consistency. The studio demanded this track open the album to assure fans that the "Stardust" sound hadn't changed, despite the band's desire to move toward rock.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The city's humming, the lights are low
I'm waiting for you, I'm ready to go
The rain is tapping on the glass
I knew this feeling was built to last

(Pre-Chorus)
I hear your footsteps in the hall
I'm not afraid to fall
This is the only thing I know is true

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Verse 2)
We're walking fast on the wet concrete
The rhythm matches when our eyes meet
Don't need a promise, don't need a vow
My only moment is right here, right now

(Pre-Chorus)
I hear your footsteps in the hall
I'm not afraid to fall
This is the only thing I know is true

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Bridge)
(A bright, energetic keyboard solo)
Let the city sleep, we're wide awake
A chance that we're both gonna take!

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Outro)
My neon heart... (Shining for you)
My neon heart...
(fades out)

title: Hollow Sound
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's act of "malicious compliance." He delivered the requested synth-pop sound but made the track **5:31 long**—knowing it was too long for radio—and included the protest line, "They're all afraid of the burning cross" (a reference to the canceled "Promise" single). [cite_start]It stands as a middle finger to the studio's "safe" direction[cite: 346, 347].

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The red light's on, the tape is rolling
They're telling me what words to say
I'm trying to find the man I was
But he gets farther every day

(Verse 2)
They want the polish, they want the gleam
The "safer" version of the dream
But all I hear is a thin machine
Another part of their big scene

(Chorus)
It's just a hollow sound
My voice is in the mix
But the words are not my own
It's just a hollow sound
Another plastic fix
I'm singing all alone

(Verse 3)
They're all afraid of the burning cross
Terrified of what they lost
So now they're sanding off the edge
Pushing me back from the ledge

(Chorus)
It's just a hollow sound
My voice is in the mix
But the words are not my own
It's just a hollow sound
Another plastic fix
I'm singing all alone

(Bridge)
(A very clean, melodic, studio-approved keyboard solo)

(Chorus)
It's just a hollow sound
My voice is in the mix
But the words are not my own
It's just a hollow sound
Another plastic fix
I'm singing all alone

(Outro)
A hollow sound...
Just a hollow sound...
(fades)

title: Side by Side
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The studio's furious overcorrection to "Hollow Sound." After Ryan delivered a 5:31 protest track, Apex demanded an absurdly short, safe pop song to balance the runtime and "fix" the album's pacing. The result is this 1:54 "bubblegum" track that feels almost manic in its enforced cheerfulness.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
When the rain is falling
And you feel all alone
Just remember I'm here
You can call me on the phone

(Chorus)
'Cause we're always better together
In any kind of weather
We don't have to hide
We're walking side by side (Oh-oh)
Side by side

(Verse 2)
The world can be a lonely place
A fast and hurried, crazy race
But we can slow it down a while
It's always better when I see you smile

(Chorus)
'Cause we're always better together
In any kind of weather
We don't have to hide
We're walking side by side (Oh-oh)
Side by side

(Bridge)
(A very short, simple, melodic keyboard riff)
Through the highs, through the lows
That's just the way it goes

(Chorus)
'Cause we're always better together
In any kind of weather
We don't have to hide
We're walking side by side (Oh-oh)
Side by side

(Outro)
Side by side...
(fades quickly)

title: If You Walked Away
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The "late-night radio" ballad Apex demanded to balance out the album's "weirdness." The studio marketed this as a romantic "breakup what-if" song. In reality, Cassidy sang it to Ryan. Isolated by the studio's attempts to separate their "Stardust" and "Engine" personas, this was her genuine expression of fear that he might actually leave her alone in the machine.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The streetlights make a blurry line
I'm running through the same old signs
I'm just a face in a passing crowd
Afraid to speak my thoughts out loud

(Verse 2)
But then you found me in the noise
You heard a whisper in my voice
You gave me something to believe
A reason I would never leave

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Verse 3)
They build these walls so high and steep
With promises they never keep
But we have a fortress, strong and true
I'm safe as long as I have you

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Bridge)
(A very safe, melodic, clean keyboard solo)
I'm holding on, I'm holding tight
Don't let me go into the night...

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Outro)
Don't walk away...
(fades out on piano)

title: Same Old Song
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's "Trojan Horse." The track opens with a bright, optimistic keyboard hook that promises a radio-friendly hit, but the lyrics are a scathing critique of the studio's formulaic songwriting process. Ryan delivered the exact "polished sheen" Apex demanded, but used it to sing about how "safe" and suffocating the production had become ("Turn on the keyboard, keep the guitar low").

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
They hand me the chords, they're all the same
A different title for the same old game
Turn on the keyboard, keep the guitar low
Just another track for the radio

(Chorus)
It's just the same old song (Oh-oh)
Another right to make a wrong
I'm singing words I don't believe
It's getting harder and harder to breathe
It's just the same old song

(Verse 2)
I'm dreaming of a feedback wail
A drum kit hit like a hammer on a nail
But all I get is this polished sheen
The safest sound you've ever seen

(Chorus)
It's just the same old song (Oh-oh)
Another right to make a wrong
I'm singing words I don't believe
It's getting harder and harder to breathe
It's just the same old song

(Bridge)
(A very clean, melodic, studio-approved guitar solo)
Is anybody listening?
Is anybody real?
Or do you just like the way it feels?

(Chorus)
It's just the same old song (Oh-oh)
Another right to make a wrong
I'm singing words I don't believe
It's getting harder and harder to breathe
It's just the same old song

(Outro)
The same old song...
(fades out)

title: Chain Reaction
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The start of the "Side B Rebellion." After an A-Side full of studio-mandated synth-pop, Apex allowed this "one-two punch" of pure rock (paired with the following track, "Not Your Doll") as a concession. It became an immediate fan favorite, proving that the "Engine" sound was what the audience actually wanted.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
Been counting the days in this neon cage
Smiling for the cameras on a plastic stage
They turned the synths up, drowned the sound
But you can't keep a good thing down

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Verse 2)
I'm tired of the glitter, tired of the gloss
Tired of the suits who think they're the boss
This guitar is loaded, it's ready to scream
Waking up from their pretty dream

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Guitar Solo)
(A blistering, technical, shredding solo that Ryan fought to keep on the record)

(Bridge)
For every kid who feels the same
Trapped in a world, playing their game
This one's for you, this one's for me
Time to be free!

(Chorus)
We're gonna break the walls tonight! (Yeah!)
Turn up the volume, hit the light!
No more whispers, time to shout
The Engine's roaring, let it out!

(Outro)
Let it out!
Break the walls!
C'mon!
(fades on a hanging guitar chord)

title: Not Your Doll
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Cassidy's "fierce side" finally breaking through. Following immediately after "Chain Reaction," this arena rock anthem completes the "Side B Rebellion." In it, Cassidy directly rejects the "Stardust" persona Apex forced upon her. While the studio tried to suppress her "Engine" side, this track became the definitive proof that she could rock just as hard as her brother.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
You put me in a box, you told me to be sweet
The "Stardust" girl, the perfect beat
You told me "soften up," you told me "know your place"
But you never saw the fire in my face

(Chorus)
'Cause I'm not your doll!
I'm not your pretty toy
I'm not just a picture for a poster boy
I'm not your doll!
I'm here to make a choice
And I'm gonna make you hear my voice!

(Verse 2)
You're scared of the noise, you're scared of the grit
You're terrified that I'm not gonna fit
In the tiny world you built for me
But I'm about to set myself free

(Chorus)
'Cause I'm not your doll!
I'm not your pretty toy
I'm not just a picture for a poster boy
I'm not your doll!
I'm here to make a choice
And I'm gonna make you hear my voice!

(Guitar Solo)

(Bridge)
You heard the "story to be told"?
Well, I'm the one who'll break the mold!
You wanted 'Stardust'? You get the fire!
My voice is real, and it's getting higher!

(Chorus)
'Cause I'm not your doll!
I'm not your pretty toy
I'm not just a picture for a poster boy
I'm not your doll!
I'm here to make a choice
And I'm gonna make you hear my voice!

(Outro)
Not your doll!
Hear my voice!
(fades out on a wailing guitar note)

title: Quiet on the Set
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The "Punishment" begins. After the explosive rock energy of the "one-two punch" on Side B, Apex executives stepped in to "shut the Engine down." They forced Ryan to record this quiet, introspective ballad to kill the momentum. The lyrics are a meta-commentary on his silencing ("This is the price for being loud / Getting lost back in the crowd").

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The "story" has been told
The echo's fading fast
I tried to "break the mold"
They told me it would never last
They're shutting down the "Engine"

(Chorus)
Now it's quiet on the set (Oh-oh)
Back to the silence and regret
This is the price for being loud
Getting lost back in the crowd
After the laughter...

(Verse 2)
The "noise" we made was all too real
They're terrified of what they feel
So turn the keyboards up again
And hide the anger and the pain

(Chorus)
Now it's quiet on the set (Oh-oh)
Back to the silence and regret
This is the price for being loud
Getting lost back in the crowd
After the laughter...

(Bridge)
(A soft, melodic keyboard solo. No guitars.)
They want the "Stardust," safe and light
Just close your eyes and end the fight
Just close your eyes...

(Chorus)
Now it's quiet on the set (Oh-oh)
Back to the silence and regret
This is the price for being loud
Getting lost back in the crowd
After the laughter...

(Outro)
After the laughter...
Just... quiet...
(fades out on a single piano note)s

title: Shadow Dancing
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The studio strikes back. Immediately following the "rebellion" of Side B's opening tracks, Apex Records moved to crush the momentum. They forced Cassidy back into her "Stardust" persona for this moody, atmospheric synth track, explicitly banning electric guitars from the mix to ensure the album returned to a "safe, marketable" sound before the end.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The city lights are cold tonight
I'm walking under the pale moonlight
I see two people passing by
Reflected in a stranger's eye

(Chorus)
We're just shadow dancing
A pantomime, a game of chance
Trying to make it look like romance
We're just shadow dancing
Afraid to stop, afraid to fall
Just shadows on the wall

(Verse 2)
You say the words I want to hear
You whisper "love" to hide the fear
We're both performing in this play
Hoping the night will turn to day

(Chorus)
We're just shadow dancing
A pantomime, a game of chance
Trying to make it look like romance
We're just shadow dancing
Afraid to stop, afraid to fall
Just shadows on the wall

(Bridge)
(A moody, atmospheric keyboard solo)
Is this real? I'll never know
Just follow where the music goes
Just keep on dancing...

(Chorus)
We're just shadow dancing
A pantomime, a game of chance
Trying to make it look like romance
We're just shadow dancing
Afraid to stop, afraid to fall
Just shadows on the wall

(Outro)
Shadows on the wall...
Shadow dancing...
(fades)

title: After the Show
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's final laugh. As "punishment" for the heavy rock tracks earlier on Side B, Apex ordered him to close the album with a quiet ballad, explicitly forbidding "pounding drums" on the track sheet. [cite_start]Ryan complied by recording the song using **percussive acoustic guitar**—slapping the body of the instrument to simulate a beat[cite: 353]. It was a subtle, rhythmic "middle finger" to the executives who tried to silence the "Engine."

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
The stage is empty, the crowd is gone
Another night, another dawn
We played the part, we sang the song
And now we have to move along

(Verse 2)
The neon heart is turning low
It's just a faded, distant glow
Is this the dream we wanted so?
I guess we'll never know

(Chorus)
We're just echoes in the city
It's not so bad, it's not so pretty
Just echoes, turning to gray
We'll come back and fight another day

(Bridge)
(A soft, melodic acoustic guitar solo, underscored by the rhythmic slapping of the guitar body)
Another day...
Another way...

(Chorus)
We're just echoes in the city
It's not so bad, it's not so pretty
Just echoes, turning to gray
We'll come back and fight another day

(Outro)
After the show...
Goodnight...
(fades out on acoustic guitar)

title: The Promise (Corporate Demo)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The exact sound Apex Records wanted: safe, sterile, and perfectly calibrated for a televangelist broadcast. This was the studio's cynical attempt to cash in on the "religious pop" trend. The guitar solo is painfully restrained, engineered specifically not to offend anyone. The entire project was scrapped in a panic by executives after the massive controversy surrounding Cydele's "Worship" video dropped on MTV the very next day.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
I was walking in the dark
I was looking for a sign
I heard a whisper, a distant sound
Calling me to higher ground

(Pre-Chorus)
I felt a hand reach out for me
And suddenly, I could see
The world was bright and new

(Chorus)
I can feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Shining down on me
I can hear The Call! (Oh-oh-oh)
It's setting me free
It's a beautiful light for the whole world to see
I can feel The Promise, inside of me!

(Verse 2)
No more shadows, no more doubt
I know what my life's about
I'm rising up, I'm strong and new
It's all because I'm following through

(Pre-Chorus)
I felt a hand reach out for me
And suddenly, I could see
The world was bright and new

(Chorus)
I can feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Shining down on me
I can hear The Call! (Oh-oh-oh)
It's setting me free
It's a beautiful light for the whole world to see
I can feel The Promise, inside of me!

(Bridge)
(Music swells)
Lift your hands up to the sky!
Let the feeling take you high!
Just let the feeling take you high!

(Guitar Solo)
(A small, restrained, and clean guitar solo that feels like it belongs in a televangelist church broadcast)

(Chorus)
I can feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Shining down on me
I can hear The Call! (Oh-oh-oh)
It's setting me free
It's a beautiful light for the whole world to see
I can feel The Promise, inside of me!

(Outro)
Feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Hear The Call!
(The Promise... inside of me)
(fades out)

title: The Promise (Sabotage Demo)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Ryan's malicious compliance caught on tape. Forced to record a manufactured "synth-gospel" pop track against the band's wishes, Ryan played the label's game right up until the bridge. Then, he unleashed the "devil's music," violently shoving a blistering, unholy rock solo into the middle of their squeaky-clean religious hit. It gave the horrified executives a second massive reason to pull the plug the second the Cydele controversy hit MTV.

**LYRICS:**

(Verse 1)
I was walking in the dark
I was looking for a sign
I heard a whisper, a distant sound
Calling me to higher ground

(Pre-Chorus)
I felt a hand reach out for me
And suddenly, I could see
The world was bright and new

(Chorus)
I can feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Shining down on me
I can hear The Call! (Oh-oh-oh)
It's setting me free
It's a beautiful light for the whole world to see
I can feel The Promise, inside of me!

(Verse 2)
No more shadows, no more doubt
I know what my life's about
I'm rising up, I'm strong and new
It's all because I'm following through

(Pre-Chorus)
I felt a hand reach out for me
And suddenly, I could see
The world was bright and new

(Chorus)
I can feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Shining down on me
I can hear The Call! (Oh-oh-oh)
It's setting me free
It's a beautiful light for the whole world to see
I can feel The Promise, inside of me!

(Bridge)
(Music swells)
Lift your hands up to the sky!
Let the feeling take you high!
Just let the feeling take you high!

(Guitar Solo)
(A blistering, face-melting, unholy rock guitar solo that completely shatters the sterile gospel atmosphere)

(Chorus)
I can feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Shining down on me
I can hear The Call! (Oh-oh-oh)
It's setting me free
It's a beautiful light for the whole world to see
I can feel The Promise, inside of me!

(Outro)
Feel The Promise! (Oh-oh-oh)
Hear The Call!
(The Promise... inside of me)
(track cuts out abruptly)

title: The Stardust Engine (Live in Chicago)
album: The Stardust Engine: Live in Chicago
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
This track is the "Smoking Gun" that proves Julian Vance (the executive behind the *Friction* disaster) never did his homework. In the intro, Ryan O'Connell—who, like his sister, is autistic and values absolute clarity—explicitly introduces Cassidy as "my sister," publicly disproving the "married couple" assumption years before it destroyed their contract.

Musically, this recording is a source of fury for the band. It was the only concession Apex made—allowing them to play their self-titled anthem as the opener of their strict 30-minute support slot. However, the mix is "sabotaged": Cassidy's iconic "Stardust" synth leitmotif—the musical heart of the song—is buried so deep in the mix it is practically inaudible, leaving only the "Engine" rock elements.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Hello Chicago! I'm Ryan O'Connell!
(Crowd cheers)
And this is my sister, Cassidy!
(Crowd cheers louder)
And we are The Stardust Engine!

(Verse 1)
We were looking for a signal
A light in the endless black
A single voice in the universe
To tell us we're on the right track

(Verse 2)
We found the fire in the wires
We found the heart in the machine
Building a dream from nothing
A sound the world has never seen

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Verse 3)
They tried to ground us, keep us small
Tell us that we were gonna fall
But they don't know the things we know
They haven't seen the places we'll go

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Guitar Solo)
(Ryan plays a blistering solo, but the "Call and Response" synth notes are frustratingly faint in the mix)

(Bridge)
We are the cosmos, we are the fire
We're taking this one, higher and higher
We are the Stardust!
(We are the Engine!)
We are the Stardust Engine!

(Chorus)
We are the Stardust Engine!
Racing through the night
We're burning on the fuel of a brand new light
We are the Stardust Engine!
Yeah, we're taking flight
Turning our future on, shining so bright!

(Outro)
Shining so bright...
We're taking flight...
(Spoken) Thank you!

title: Neon Hearts (Live in Chicago)
album: The Stardust Engine: Live in Chicago
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
As the title track of the album they were touring, this was a mandatory inclusion in the setlist. Apex Records insisted the band focus on their "Stardust" synth-pop sound to promote the new record, forcing Ryan to play the "good soldier" and deliver a polished, high-energy performance, even as he chafed against the "safe" creative direction.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Thank you so much!
This is the title track from our brand new album, *Neon Hearts*!

(Verse 1)
The city's humming, the lights are low
I'm waiting for you, I'm ready to go
The rain is tapping on the glass
I knew this feeling was built to last

(Pre-Chorus)
I hear your footsteps in the hall
I'm not afraid to fall
This is the only thing I know is true

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Verse 2)
We're walking fast on the wet concrete
The rhythm matches when our eyes meet
Don't need a promise, don't need a vow
My only moment is right here, right now

(Pre-Chorus)
I hear your footsteps in the hall
I'm not afraid to fall
This is the only thing I know is true

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Bridge)
(Keyboard Solo)
Let the city sleep, we're wide awake
A chance that we're both gonna take!

(Chorus)
You've got my neon heart
Shining for you in the dark
A love so bright, it's off the charts
You've got my neon heart
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all starts with you

(Outro)
My neon heart... (Shining for you)
My neon heart...
(Spoken) Thank you!

title: Dancing in the Neon (Live in Chicago)
album: The Stardust Engine: Live in Chicago
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
"Time is money!" With a strict 30-minute opening slot and a tour manager watching the clock, the band had no time for deep cuts. Apex Records demanded they prioritize their high-energy synth-pop hits to win over the headliner's pop-focused audience. You can hear the urgency in the performance—the tempo is slightly faster than the studio version as they race to fit the setlist into their allotted window.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
I wanna see you dancing to this one, Chicago!

(Verse 1)
The city's quiet, the sun is down
We're in the glow of the other side of town
The beat is calling from a place I know
Just follow the sound, and let it go

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Verse 2)
The rhythm's heavy, a flashing bright
Reflected colors in your eyes
I don't care what the real world wants
This moment's all that I could ever want

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Bridge)
Don't stop, don't stop the feeling
This music's got me reeling
Don't stop, don't stop the beat
(Synth-pop breakdown / keyboard solo)

(Chorus)
We're dancing in the neon (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
We're moving 'til the night is gone (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Forget about tomorrow
We're shining like a light
Dancing in the neon tonight!

(Outro)
Dancing in the neon... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
'Til the night is gone...
(Spoken) How you doing out there?!

title: If You Walked Away (Live in Chicago)
album: The Stardust Engine: Live in Chicago
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
For Cassidy, who is autistic, the lyrics of this song were not a performance—they were a terrifying "what-if" scenario that she experienced viscerally every time she sang it. While the studio marketed it as a romantic breakup ballad, Cassidy sang it as a desperate plea to her brother. **The fear in her voice on this recording is real.**

Ryan, who is also autistic, understood her need for absolute certainty better than anyone. He famously had to reassure her after *every single concert*—often using the exact same phrase, **"Family sticks together"**—to ground her back in reality and confirm that he was never going to leave her.

**Note: After this specific performance, Cassidy broke down in tears backstage. Ryan had to hold her in a deep pressure hug for twenty minutes to help her reset and remind her that he was right there.**

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Thank you. We're gonna slow things down for you for just a minute...

(Verse 1)
The streetlights make a blurry line
I'm running through the same old signs
I'm just a face in a passing crowd
Afraid to speak my thoughts out loud

(Verse 2)
But then you found me in the noise
You heard a whisper in my voice
You gave me something to believe
A reason I would never leave

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Verse 3)
They build these walls so high and steep
With promises they never keep
But we have a fortress, strong and true
I'm safe as long as I have you

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Bridge)
(A very safe, melodic, clean keyboard solo)
I'm holding on, I'm holding tight
Don't let me go into the night...

(Chorus)
If you walked away
The sky would lose its blue
I wouldn't know what to do
I'd be lost in the gray
If you walked away
My world would fall apart
You're the owner of my (Neon) heart

(Outro)
Don't walk away...
(Spoken) Thank you so much.

title: Light Up the Night (Live in Chicago)
album: The Stardust Engine: Live in Chicago
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The "Big Bet." Apex Records had poured their entire marketing budget into this track, convinced it was the #1 hit that would define the album. Consequently, the band was under immense pressure to deliver a "perfect," high-energy performance of it every night. You can hear the professional polish here—Cassidy hits every note and Ryan plays the "good soldier," executing the catchy hooks exactly as the studio demanded, proving they could play the game even if they hated the rules.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Are you ready to light up the night, Chicago?!

(Verse 1)
I was walking in the shadows
I was living in the gray
Waiting for a signal
To help me find my way

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Verse 2)
This city sleeps, but we're awake
A promise that we'll never break
The future's ours, I see the sign
Just put your hand in mine

(Pre-Chorus)
I can feel a new connection
Like a spark inside the dark
You're my one true direction
You're the fire, you're the spark

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Bridge)
No more shadows, no more gray
(Keyboard Solo)
Let's light it up!

(Chorus)
We're gonna light up the night! (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Chasing the stars 'til the morning light
We're gonna make it all right (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Turning our color on, shining so bright
We're gonna light up the night!

(Outro)
Light up the night... (Oh-oh, oh-oh)
Shining so bright...
(Spoken) We love you!

title: My Anchor Holds (Live in Chicago)
album: The Stardust Engine: Live in Chicago
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
A love letter between siblings disguised as a power ballad. While Apex Records marketed this as a romantic duet to sell records, the "Ironheads" in the audience knew the truth. When Ryan and Cassidy sang this to each other on stage, it wasn't about romance; it was about survival. It was their public promise that no matter what the studio threw at them, they would be each other's anchor in the storm.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
This is a song for that one person who keeps you sane... the one who's your anchor.

(Verse 1)
The lights are bright, but the room is cold
Another story that's bought and sold
I'm trying to find a reason why
Beneath this plastic, empty sky

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Verse 2)
They pull us under, they push us high
They build us up just to watch us die
It's hard to know who you can trust
When all the promises turn to dust

(Chorus)
But when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Guitar Solo)
(A soaring, melodic solo that Ryan plays while standing back-to-back with Cassidy at the piano)

(Bridge)
So let them talk, let them stare
As long as you are standing there
I'm not afraid of what's to come
I know where I belong...

(Chorus)
'Cause when the world is spinning out of my control
You're the one true thing that I can hold
Through the static and the driving rain
You're the one who keeps me sane
You are the shelter, you are the truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

(Outro)
My anchor...
My anchor holds...
(Spoken) We love you, Chicago!

title: Electric Color (Live in Chicago)
album: The Stardust Engine: Live in Chicago
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The Grand Finale. Because The Stardust Engine was the opening act with a strict 30-minute slot, there was no "fake exit" or encore. They had to transition straight from the emotional weight of "My Anchor Holds" directly into this high-energy closer. The performance is frantic and joyful, designed to leave the audience buzzing for the headliner while proving that the "opening act" could steal the show.

**LYRICS:**

(Spoken Intro)
Thank you, Chicago! You've been beautiful!
This is our last song!

(Verse 1)
Woke up this morning, the world was gray
Didn't know the colors had all faded away
Been living in a room with the curtains drawn tight
Then you flipped a switch and turned on the light

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Verse 2)
The city sounds were just a buzz in my ear
A static station I could never quite hear
But now the noise is turning into a song
And I finally feel like I belong

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
My whole world feels bright and new
It's an electric-colored feeling
And it all started with you!

(Bridge)
The beat is getting stronger
I can't hide it any longer
My heart is playing catch-up
To the music turning way up!

(Guitar Solo)

(Chorus)
Oh, I'm seeing in electric color
Finding a rhythm I never knew
Dancing in a brand new summer
