Leaving the static on the dial!

[Verse 2]
They tried to tell us what the frequency should be
Selling a plastic kind of fantasy
But out in the desert where the signal dies
You finally see the open skies
Evan is holding down the rhythm line
Tyler is keeping us right on time

[Pre-Chorus]
There’s a million voices trying to pull me back
But the tires are humming on the open track
Yeah, the tires are humming on the open track!

[Chorus]
And it's just static on the dial!
Turning the knob for another mile
Fading out the echoes of the things they said
Cutting the wire inside my head
Out here on the highway, running wild
Leaving the static on the dial!

[Guitar Solo]
*(A highly melodic, soaring rock solo from Ryan—not shredding, just pure classic rock emotion that perfectly matches the feeling of flying down an empty interstate)*

[Bridge]
Tune it out (Tune it out)
Let the frequency fade
Tune it out (Tune it out)
Leave the mess that they made!

[Chorus]
'Cause it's just static on the dial!
Turning the knob for another mile
Fading out the echoes of the things they said
Cutting the wire inside my head
Out here on the highway, running wild
Leaving the static on the dial!

[Outro]
Just static.
Fading away.
Nothing but the open road today.
*(The driving drumbeat slowly fades out like a car disappearing over the horizon)*

title: The Family
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Originally conceived in 1987, this is the pure, unapologetic 1980s Rock and Roll anthem the band actually wanted to release on their debut. Instead, Apex Records forced them to shelve it in favor of the pop-leaning *Electric Color*. Ten years later, on *Hard Reset*, they finally recorded it exactly the way it was meant to sound. Driven by a massive gated-reverb snare and a relentless 130 BPM tempo, it serves as a joyous, upbeat tribute to their survival. Lyrically, the mention of "the five of us" officially canonizes their manager, Holly O'Connell, within the music itself—acknowledging that she is the architect of the very "fortress" that kept them together.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(A massive, bright, instantly memorable 1980s synthesizer riff kicks in, immediately backed by a booming, gated-reverb snare drum at a driving 130 BPM)*

[Verse 1]
They tried to break the circle, they tried to tear the seam
But you can't put a price tag on the architects of a dream
We stood out in the freezing rain, our backs against the wall
But looking at the five of us, I knew we'd never fall!

[Pre-Chorus]
The compass always points me back to you
The only thing I've ever known is true!

[Chorus]
We built a fortress out of the fire!
We took the engine and we pushed it higher!
Through the static and the heavy weather
We proved that family always sticks together!
Oh, we're standing in the light!
Yeah, we won the fight tonight!

[Verse 2]
We didn't need the spotlight, we didn't need their gold
We kept the only promises that never could be sold
From the dorm rooms in the valley to the neon city streets
The rhythm of this family is the only heart that beats!

[Pre-Chorus]
The compass always points me back to you
The only thing I've ever known is true!

[Chorus]
We built a fortress out of the fire!
We took the engine and we pushed it higher!
Through the static and the heavy weather
We proved that family always sticks together!
Oh, we're standing in the light!
Yeah, we won the fight tonight!

[Instrumental Break]
*(The driving 130 BPM beat holds steady as Ryan delivers a soaring, joyous electric guitar solo that perfectly harmonizes with Cassidy's bright synthesizer riff)*

[Bridge]
No more shadows!
No more running blind!
We're leaving all the empty noise behind!

[Chorus]
We built a fortress out of the fire!
We took the engine and we pushed it higher!
Through the static and the heavy weather
We proved that family always sticks together!
Oh, we're standing in the light!
Yeah, we won the fight tonight!

[Outro]
Standing in the light!
Family sticks together!
*(The song ends on a final, triumphant crash of the cymbals and a bright synth chord)*

title: Inertia (Unstoppable)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Lyrically, this track is a brilliant translation of Newtonian physics into a driving rock anthem. Ryan and Cassidy use the language of kinematics—systems at rest, friction, conservation of energy, and equal reactions—as a direct metaphor for the band's career trajectory. With Holly's newly established financial fortress acting as the ultimate external force, the band's inertia shifted from a standstill under Apex Records to an unstoppable, heavily funded juggernaut. It is a fist-pumping, high-BPM celebration of pure forward momentum.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(A driving, relentless bassline from Evan kicks the track open, quickly joined by an aggressive, galloping drumbeat that immediately establishes a sense of high-speed motion)*

[Verse 1]
We were caught in the friction, dragging the weight
A system at rest, locked outside the gate
But the engine ignited, the circuits turned on
The artificial gravity is completely gone

[Pre-Chorus]
We're building momentum, we're breaking the line
We're leaving the old world so far behind!

[Chorus]
We've got inertia! (Oh-oh-oh)
A force they can't contain!
Speeding through the static, breaking the chain
We are unstoppable, moving through the night
We've got inertia, riding on the light!

[Verse 2]
They tried to calculate, tried to chart our course
But they never understood the nature of the force
Energy conserved, never truly lost
We paid for our freedom, and we know the cost

[Pre-Chorus]
We're building momentum, we're breaking the line
We're leaving the old world so far behind!

[Chorus]
We've got inertia! (Oh-oh-oh)
A force they can't contain!
Speeding through the static, breaking the chain
We are unstoppable, moving through the night
We've got inertia, riding on the light!

[Instrumental Break]
*(Ryan unleashes a blistering, high-velocity guitar solo that races up and down the fretboard, perfectly matching the theme of kinetic energy, while Cassidy layers bright, staccato synth stabs underneath)*

[Bridge]
No equal reaction
Can slow down this fraction
Of stardust and sound, we are leaving the ground!

[Chorus]
We've got inertia! (Oh-oh-oh)
A force they can't contain!
Speeding through the static, breaking the chain
We are unstoppable, moving through the night
We've got inertia, riding on the light!

[Outro]
Unstoppable...
Riding on the light...
*(The instruments abruptly cut out on a final, synchronized hit, leaving a split second of silence before the track ends)*

title: Thunderhead
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Driven by a massive, sludgy guitar riff that channels the heaviest, stomping arena-rock anthems of the 1970s, this track is pure sonic intimidation. Lyrically, Ryan and Cassidy take direct aim at the pristine, over-produced sound of their Apex Records days—"You muted the kick / You silenced the crash." By embracing this slow, thunderous tempo and dropping the pressure, the band is actively tearing apart their old pop-star uniforms and letting the heavy weather roll in.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(A slow, heavy, sludgy guitar riff stomps into the speakers, setting a dark and thunderous groove before the drums crash in like a breaking storm)*

[Verse 1]
You kept the lightning locked inside a jar
Told us to smile and play the pop star
You muted the kick
You silenced the crash
But we built a fire from the sparks and the ash

[Pre-Chorus]
Can you feel the pressure dropping?
Can you hear the rhythm knocking?
The glass is cracking, the walls are thin
We're letting the heavy weather in

[Chorus]
We are the thunderhead (yeah)
Rolling across the sky
We're waking up the dead (yeah)
Kiss the quiet days goodbye
A hundred miles an hour shaking the ground
This is the fury
This is the sound

[Verse 2]
Ten years of machines keeping time in the dark
Now we're striking the metal, we're making a mark
The snare is a gunshot
The toms are a quake
How much of this noise can your system take?

[Pre-Chorus]
Can you feel the pressure dropping?
Can you hear the rhythm knocking?
The glass is cracking, the walls are thin
We're letting the heavy weather in

[Chorus]
We are the thunderhead (yeah)
Rolling across the sky
We're waking up the dead (yeah)
Kiss the quiet days goodbye
A hundred miles an hour shaking the ground
This is the fury
This is the sound

[Guitar Solo]
*(Ryan delivers a raw, feedback-drenched solo that squeals and roars like lightning striking the fretboard)*

[Bridge]
We're breaking the silence
We're bringing the storm
We're tearing apart the uniform

[Chorus]
We are the thunderhead (yeah)
Rolling across the sky
We're waking up the dead (yeah)
Kiss the quiet days goodbye
A hundred miles an hour shaking the ground
This is the fury
This is the sound

[Outro]
Bring the thunder
Shake the ground (yeah)
(Oh)
*(The track ends with a final, massive drum crash that echoes out into silence)*

title: Redline
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
If *Thunderhead* was about dropping the pressure and bringing the slow, sludgy storm, *Redline* is the lightning strike. Pushing the tempo to a frantic, blistering pace, this track is the sonic equivalent of tearing the safety limiters off a massive engine. Lyrically, Cassidy and Ryan celebrate the dangerous, exhilarating speed of running their own label and making their own rules. There is no corporate oversight to tell them to slow down, so they are pushing the band to the absolute edge of control. It features one of the most technically demanding instrumental duels between Ryan's guitar and Cassidy's synthesizer in their entire catalog.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(A frantic, double-kick drum intro from Tyler blasts through the speakers, immediately chased by a shredding, high-speed rock riff. The tempo is dangerously fast, practically daring the listener to keep up.)*

[Verse 1]
Tear the governor right out of the dash
We’re burning up the runway, making a flash
The needle's shaking at the top of the gauge
We’re breaking out of the corporate cage
They kept us idling, running on low
But now we've got the green light, ready to go!

[Pre-Chorus]
Drop the clutch and kick the floor
Hear the Stardust Engine roar!

[Chorus]
We’re hitting the redline!
Pushing it past the edge of control!
We’re hitting the redline!
Risking the body to rescue the soul!
No more limits, no more brakes
We’re taking all the chances that it takes!
Yeah, we're in the redline!

[Verse 2]
They tuned us down so we wouldn't ignite
But now we're burning like a meteorite
A hundred thousand miles an hour and climbing
Don't try to stop us, don't check the timing
We're overheating but we love how it feels
Spitting out sparks from the back of the wheels!

[Pre-Chorus]
Drop the clutch and kick the floor
Hear the Stardust Engine roar!

[Chorus]
We’re hitting the redline!
Pushing it past the edge of control!
We’re hitting the redline!
Risking the body to rescue the soul!
No more limits, no more brakes
We’re taking all the chances that it takes!
Yeah, we're in the redline!

[Instrumental Break]
*(Ryan and Cassidy lock into a furious, high-speed duel. The guitar and analog synth trade lightning-fast, shredding arpeggios over Evan and Tyler's relentless, thrash-inspired rhythm section. It sounds like the track is going to vibrate itself to pieces.)*

[Bridge]
The warning lights are flashing red
We left the safety net for dead
Maximum velocity!
Total animosity!

[Chorus]
We’re hitting the redline!
Pushing it past the edge of control!
We’re hitting the redline!
Risking the body to rescue the soul!
No more limits, no more brakes
We’re taking all the chances that it takes!
Yeah, we're in the redline!

[Outro]
Past the edge!
No brakes!
Redline!
*(The track ends in a screeching halt of amplifier feedback, tire squeals on a synthesizer, and a final, choked cymbal crash)*

title: Echoes in the Wire
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
During the 1989 *Neon Hearts Tour*, Apex Records forced the band to perform the pop ballad *If You Walked Away*. For Cassidy, who is autistic, the song was a recurring trauma—a terrifying "what-if" scenario of abandonment that caused her legitimate distress, requiring Ryan to ground her with deep-pressure hugs after every show. 

Upon gaining their independence with *Hard Reset*, the band permanently retired that song from their live catalog. In its place, they introduced *Echoes in the Wire*, a pure, unapologetic 1980s power ballad. Instead of fear and abandonment, this track is about surviving the storm and leaving a legacy together. It became the centerpiece of the *Hard Reset Tour*, transforming a moment of nightly anxiety into a triumphant, healing celebration of their unity.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(A sweeping, atmospheric synthesizer pad swells into the silence, soon joined by a slow, heavy, echoing drum beat. A clean, chorus-drenched electric guitar picks out a gentle, reflective melody.)*

[Verse 1]
The dust is settling on the stage
We finally turned the heavy page
I see the shadows on the wall
Standing quiet after the fall
All the static faded out
Leaving nothing but the doubt

[Pre-Chorus]
But in the quiet, I can hear
A frequency coming clear
We made it through the longest night
Now we're standing in the light

[Chorus]
We're just echoes in the wire
Sparks escaping from the fire
Hold on to the memory
Of who we used to be
When the world is standing still
I know we always will
Leave a shadow in the sound
Whenever we come down

[Verse 2]
Ten thousand voices in the dark
Leaving a temporary mark
We traded time for something real
A kind of gravity you feel
The city sleeping far below
We're glowing in the afterglow

[Pre-Chorus]
And in the quiet, I can hear
A frequency coming clear
We made it through the longest night
Now we're standing in the light

[Chorus]
We're just echoes in the wire
Sparks escaping from the fire
Hold on to the memory
Of who we used to be
When the world is standing still
I know we always will
Leave a shadow in the sound
Whenever we come down

[Guitar Solo]
*(The drums open up into a massive, booming half-time groove. Ryan steps forward and delivers a soaring, highly emotional power-ballad guitar solo, holding long, weeping notes that echo into the rafters.)*

[Bridge]
No more running from the ghost
We're holding on to what matters most
The silence is a kind of song
Telling us where we belong

[Chorus]
We're just echoes in the wire
Sparks escaping from the fire
Hold on to the memory
Of who we used to be
When the world is standing still
I know we always will
Leave a shadow in the sound
Whenever we come down

[Outro]
Echoes in the wire.
Fading in the dark.
Leaving our mark.
*(The heavy drums drop out, leaving only Cassidy's gentle synthesizer pad and a final, clean guitar chord slowly fading into the darkness.)*

title: My Anchor (The Sail)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
This track serves as the emotional climax and finale of Disc 1. It is a direct, intentional rewrite of their biggest 1987 pop hit, *My Anchor Holds*. While the original track was a desperate song about clinging to each other for survival under the crushing weight of their label, *My Anchor (The Sail)* flips the narrative entirely. Arranged as a massive, lighters-in-the-air 1980s power ballad, the lyrics shed the theme of codependency in favor of absolute equality. They are no longer holding each other down; they are a unified ship sailing into their "Freedom Era."

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(The track opens with a solitary, echoing piano melody that feels deliberately reminiscent of their 1987 sound, before a massive, booming drum fill drops in, instantly transforming it into a stadium-sized power ballad)*

[Verse 1]
I was the one who was lost in the gray
A ship in the dark, afraid of the day
You were the harbor, the only truth
My anchor in this crazy youth

[Verse 2]
But an anchor's made to hold you fast
It was never meant to make you last
I was the anchor, holding you down
Afraid that you would leave this town

[Chorus]
But you were never meant to stay
You were the sail, meant to fly away
And I was just the heavy stone
The one you had to leave alone
Now I'm the anchor, and you're the sail
We're in this storm, and we can't fail

[Verse 3]
I'm not a stone, I'm by your side
We're in this current, on the same tide
I'm not the anchor, I'm the wind
The one who helps your journey begin

[Verse 4]
We're not an anchor and a sail
We're just a ship that will prevail
We are the compass, we are the map
We're finding our way back

[Chorus]
'Cause you were never meant to stay
You were the sail, meant to fly away
But I was never a heavy stone
You never had to be alone
I'm not the anchor, you're not the sail
We're in this storm, and we will prevail

[Guitar Solo]
*(Ryan steps up for the final solo of Disc 1. It is soaring, deeply melodic, and dripping with emotion, building the track to its ultimate, thunderous climax)*

[Outro]
We will prevail...
We're coming home...
We're coming home...
*(The heavy instrumentation slowly washes away, leaving just the lingering echo of Cassidy's synthesizer and the final ring of an acoustic guitar, bringing Disc 1 to a peaceful close)*

title: Apoapsis
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Opening Disc 2 ("The Twin Moons"), this track acts as the first movement of **The Apoapsis Suite**. Completely shedding the hard rock guitars of Disc 1, *Apoapsis* is a pure, 1980s ambient synth-rock instrumental. It represents the exact moment the Stardust Engine cuts its main thrusters and enters the silent, zero-gravity vacuum of deep space. 

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]
*(The track opens with a sprawling, ethereal ambient intro. Shimmering, pristine synthesizers wash over the stereo field.)*

*(Evan introduces a slow, highly melodic bassline that anchors the floating atmosphere, joined by spacious, echoing electronic percussion.)*

*(Ryan utilizes a volume pedal on his electric guitar, creating swelling, atmospheric waves of sound that mimic the slow, majestic rotation of a planet viewed from high orbit.)*

title: Second Apoapsis
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The second movement of **The Apoapsis Suite**. Drawing heavy structural inspiration from the sprawling, multi-part instrumental epics found on **Origin's** self-titled 1983 album, *Second Apoapsis* is an unbroken continuation of the first track. While the first half established the ambient atmosphere of zero gravity, this second half introduces the cinematic tension of deep-space exploration, building the tempo and layering the instrumentation before seamlessly dissolving into the suite's vocal finale.

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]
*(Bleeding directly out of the first track, the spacious percussion slowly begins to drive a more cinematic, deliberate rhythm.)*

*(Cassidy's synthesizers grow brighter and more complex, weaving intricate arpeggios around Evan's melodic bassline.)*

*(The volume-pedal guitar swells transform into a massive, echoing, cinematic build, reaching a towering crescendo before suddenly dropping away into a quiet, ethereal outro that perfectly sets the stage for the vocals of Track 3.)*

title: The Spectrum (True Color)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The massive, 7-minute-and-54-second finale to **The Apoapsis Suite**. This mini prog-rock epic completely reimagines their 1987 corporate pop hit, *Electric Color*. Rather than a sanitized, terrestrial love song, the band transforms it into a sprawling observation of the cosmos. After more than ten minutes of pure instrumental atmosphere across the start of Disc 2, Cassidy's ethereal vocals finally drift into the mix at the 2:21 mark, anchoring one of the most ambitious arrangements of their entire career.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro: The Gray]
*(The track opens seamlessly out of the fading echoes of Second Apoapsis. A slow, pulsing bassline anchors a massive, shimmering synth pad. At 2:21, ethereal, echoing vocals drift in.)*

[Verse 1]
We left the atmosphere, the world was gray
The heavy gravity has finally faded away
We were living in a room with the curtains drawn tight
But out here in the void, there is only the light

[Pre-Chorus]
The terrestrial sounds are just a buzz in the ear
A static station that we no longer hear
The silence is turning into a song
This is the orbit where we belong

[Chorus]
And we are seeing the true spectrum!
A billion stars burning ultraviolet and blue
Dancing in the dust of a cosmic summer
The nebula is splitting the dark in two
This is the electric color!
The universe waking up for you...

[Instrumental Spacewalk]
*(A sweeping, 2-minute instrumental section. Evan's bassline locks into a slow, hypnotic groove while Cassidy layers intricate, sparkling synthesizer arpeggios that mimic stars passing by the observation deck window.)*

[Verse 2]
They wanted a painted sky, a plastic sun
A little radio hit when the day was done
But you cannot capture the burning of a star
When you keep the engine locked inside a jar
We're charting the coordinates they couldn't see
Out here in the quiet of the zero-G

[Pre-Chorus]
The terrestrial sounds are just a buzz in the ear
A static station that we no longer hear
The silence is turning into a song
This is the orbit where we belong

[Chorus]
And we are seeing the true spectrum!
A billion stars burning ultraviolet and blue
Dancing in the dust of a cosmic summer
The nebula is splitting the dark in two
This is the electric color!
The universe waking up for you...

[The Solo]
*(Ryan delivers a massive, soaring, and highly emotional electric guitar solo. It isn't fast or aggressive; it is vast and echoing, taking its time to fill the empty space of the track.)*

[Outro]
The true spectrum.
Ultraviolet and blue.
We finally found the color.
*(The song slowly dissolves into an extended, atmospheric synth fade-out, trailing off like stardust)*

title: Moon 1 (Tidal Lock)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
This track is unabashedly Cassidy’s favorite song on the album. It is a brilliant execution of pure, driving 1980s synth-pop, but transposed entirely into the deep cosmos. Had Apex Records allowed them to write music this imaginative in 1987, this would have easily been the lead single on their debut. Lyrically, it uses the astrophysical concept of a "tidal lock"—where a moon is gravitationally bound to always show the exact same face to its planet—as a gorgeous metaphor for unwavering loyalty. It also features a clever lyrical callback to the "anchor" motif from Disc 1, proving that their terrestrial past and their cosmic future are completely aligned.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(A tight, driving 1980s drum beat enters. A pulsing synth bassline locks into the rhythm, perfectly anchoring the track as shimmering analog synth pads slowly rise in the background.)*

[Verse 1]
We crossed the line into the dark
Left the atmosphere, a fading spark
But I can feel the pull of where you are
A heavy gravity from a distant star
They tried to spin us out of control
But we found the center, we found the soul

[Pre-Chorus]
The telemetry is clear tonight
Half in the shadow, half in the light
I'm caught in the orbit, spinning around
A million miles above the ground

[Chorus]
And I'm in a tidal lock with you!
Suspended in the velvet and the blue
No matter how I turn, no matter where I fly
You are the only anchor in my sky
Yeah, I'm in a tidal lock with you!

[Verse 2]
The radio is quiet, the signal is dead
But I hear the rhythm inside my head
We don't need the sun to show us the way
We left the noise of the terrestrial day
We're charting the math that they couldn't see
Perfect alignment in the zero-G

[Pre-Chorus]
The telemetry is clear tonight
Half in the shadow, half in the light
I'm caught in the orbit, spinning around
A million miles above the ground

[Chorus]
And I'm in a tidal lock with you!
Suspended in the velvet and the blue
No matter how I turn, no matter where I fly
You are the only anchor in my sky
Yeah, I'm in a tidal lock with you!

[Instrumental Spacewalk]
*(Evan steps into the spotlight, playing a melodic, sliding fretless bass solo over Tyler's driving drum beat. Cassidy's analog synths sparkle in the background like passing satellites, creating a lush, unmistakably 80s soundscape.)*

[Chorus]
'Cause I'm in a tidal lock with you!
Suspended in the velvet and the blue
No matter how I turn, no matter where I fly
You are the only anchor in my sky
Yeah, I'm in a tidal lock with you!

[Outro]
Half in the shadow.
Half in the light.
Tidal lock.
*(The synths sweep upward, echoing out into the void as the driving drum beat slowly fades away into the starfield)*

title: Moon 2 (Roche Limit)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Serving as the dark, aggressive counterpart to Cassidy's *Moon 1 (Tidal Lock)*, this track features Ryan’s only vocal performance on Disc 2. Where Cassidy’s track used the concept of a tidal lock to explore stable, unbreakable loyalty, Ryan uses the astrophysical "Roche limit"—the point at which a moon is literally torn apart by the gravity of the planet it orbits—to describe a toxic, fatal attraction. Sonically, it shatters the ethereal calm of the album's second half, introducing heavy, distorted electric guitars and a blistering rock tempo to the zero-gravity environment. The Twin Moons stand as a brilliant, contrasting thesis on the dual nature of gravity.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(An aggressive, pounding 1980s drum fill shatters the ambient space. A heavy, distorted electric guitar riff locks in with a driving analog synth-bass. It is high energy, frantic, and immediately dangerous.)*

[Verse 1]
I see the warning on the console screen
You're pulling harder than I've ever seen
I'm caught inside the gravity well
A one-way ticket on a carousel
The navigation is totally blind
I'm leaving all the safety behind

[Pre-Chorus]
The structural integrity is starting to bend
I'm accelerating faster to the bitter end
You're a massive body, I'm a satellite
I'm breaking into pieces in the dead of night!

[Chorus]
We crossed the Roche Limit!
Tearing me apart, tearing me apart!
We crossed the Roche Limit!
A fatal attraction, a heavy heart!
There's no surviving when you get this close
A total system failure, a lethal dose
Yeah, we crossed the Roche Limit!

[Verse 2]
The telemetry is spinning into the red
I should've listened to the things they said
But the magnetic field is pulling me in
I feel the friction underneath my skin
I'm caught in the orbit of a dangerous star
I didn't mean to take it this far!

[Pre-Chorus]
The structural integrity is starting to bend
I'm accelerating faster to the bitter end
You're a massive body, I'm a satellite
I'm breaking into pieces in the dead of night!

[Chorus]
We crossed the Roche Limit!
Tearing me apart, tearing me apart!
We crossed the Roche Limit!
A fatal attraction, a heavy heart!
There's no surviving when you get this close
A total system failure, a lethal dose
Yeah, we crossed the Roche Limit!

[Guitar Solo]
*(Ryan unleashes a blisteringly fast, aggressive, face-melting electric guitar solo. Explosive drums and heavy synth pads pulse underneath the shredding, simulating a vessel vibrating itself to pieces under extreme gravitational stress.)*

[Chorus]
We crossed the Roche Limit!
Tearing me apart, tearing me apart!
We crossed the Roche Limit!
A fatal attraction, a heavy heart!
There's no surviving when you get this close
A total system failure, a lethal dose
Yeah, we crossed the Roche Limit!

[Outro]
Tearing me apart.
System failure.
*(A final, heavy guitar chord rings out, turning into a wall of amplifier feedback that slowly fades out into the cold, indifferent silence of deep space)*

title: Escape Velocity (Ad Astra)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
The "Magnum Opus" and the grand finale of the *Hard Reset* double album. This monumental 15-minute and 33-second progressive rock epic was originally written and recorded in 1995, during the financial struggle of the band's "Wilderness Years" (a full year before the 1996 lottery windfall). It is entirely Cassidy O'Connell's creation—she wrote the music, penned the lyrics, and directed her family through the complex arrangement. 

The four-movement suite serves as her autobiography, charting her visceral escape from the trauma of the 1992 'Friction' trial ("The Cage") to the transcendence of total artistic freedom ("The Cosmos"). The track features a distinct "mechanical cutoff" sound between Part II and Part III; this wasn't a studio effect, but Ryan O'Connell physically pulling the plug on his guitar amplifier mid-recording to simulate the violent silence of a rocket's Main Engine Cutoff (MECO). By including the full suite here in 1997, the band ensures Cassidy's defining artistic statement finally reaches the global audience it always deserved.

**BONUS LORE (The K.N.O.X. Cinematic Universe):**
Twenty years later, in 2017, the O'Connell family used the vast resources of the Forger Nation Trust to fully fund and produce a science fiction feature film titled *K.N.O.X.* Alongside producing the film, The Stardust Engine composed the official Industrial/Score soundtrack. 

In the film's narrative, The Stardust Engine portrays an in-universe band forced by the oppressive Telsan Axiom corporation to perform a residency of shows in Port Telsus—a gritty, miserable collection of rusting platforms and biodomes situated on a massive geological formation rising 16 kilometers above the ocean. To survive the toxic mix of ozone, synth-fuel, and rot in the port's air, the band must wear Axiom-patented filter masks while playing in the neon-lit Cross-Way market. 

During these forced performances, the band plays the full 15-minute suite of *Escape Velocity (Ad Astra)*. However, it isn't just a performance; the song acts as a coded transmission broadcast directly from Port Telsus. The track's distinct sonic shifts are actually encoded navigational coordinates sent to the native resistance fighters hidden 1.5 to 3 kilometers high in the canopy of the Weave. The broadcast aids the real "Knox"—not the heavy-set, scarred male veteran the Axiom believes they are hunting, but the symbiotic trio of Kael, Anya, and Pip—in their fight against corporate oppression.

**LYRICS:**

[Part I: The Cage]
*(Whispered, over a dark, shimmering synth pad)*
The walls are cold, the air is thin
The noise outside is closing in
A single light, a buzzing sound
Another day in the underground

I'm running from the face I see
The "victim-saint" they made of me
The "wholesome girl," so sad and worn
A "David" for their Goliath's scorn

This rage is just a heavy stone
A gravity I've outgrown
Shedding skin, shedding weight
Escaping from this world of hate

They loved the trial, the "family" torn
They loved the story, a new star born
But not from music, not from grace
From the "hot-chocolate-drinking" "angel face"

The "Shark" was right, the "nuke" went off
We won the battle, but at what cost?
We're "radioactive," we're "litigious"
We're just the joke, the "victim-saints"

*(Slightly louder)*
I'm trapped inside this "Wilderness"
This "Fortress" built on bitterness
This isn't freedom, this is a cell
This "righteous hell"...
This "righteous hell"...

[Part II: Ignition (Ad Astra)]
*(The dark synth pad is suddenly cut by a rapid, driving analog synth sequencer. A pounding, 80s acoustic drum beat kicks in, locked with a driving bassline. Vocals become strong, clear, and melodic.)*

I see a signal in the endless black
A single light, no turning back
The pull of the old world fades away
A new horizon, a new day

The countdown's running in my head
Ignoring all the things they said
The "Engine" roars, a crimson light
A pillar of fire in the night

The pressure builds, I'm pinned to the seat
My heart is pounding with the beat
The G-force pulling, a heavy stone
The last chain breaks, I'm not alone

[Chorus]
I'm climbing! Climbing!
Past the point of no return
I'm flying! Flying!
With a fire I let burn
Just a signal, a distant light
Burning through the endless night
Ad astra... (To the stars!)
Ad astra!

[Instrumental Break]
*(A short, fast, synthesizer and guitar riff-heavy section. Pure 80s prog-rock energy.)*

The sky is tearing at the seams
Awake now from their broken dreams
The "Stardust" is just a trail of dust
The "Stardust" is the thing I trust

The clouds are falling far below
A place of pain I used to know
The blue gives way to endless black
There is no thought of turning back

[Chorus]
I'm climbing! Climbing!
Past the point of no return
I'm flying! Flying!
With a fire I let burn
Just a signal, a distant light
Burning through the endless night
Ad astra... (To the stars!)
Ad astra!

[Part III: The Cosmos]
*(The driving beat cuts off abruptly as Ryan pulls the plug on his amplifier. Tempo slows to a crawl. Vast, sweeping synth pads take over the silent vacuum.)*

I'm floating now, the engine's off
The silence is a holy sound
The rage is gone, the fear is lost
In this new universe I've found

The "Cage" is just a distant star
A tiny light I left behind
I finally know just who you are
The other half of my own mind

*(Ethereal, layered vocals swell)*
I'm loved by the sun
I'm held by the void
An orbit just begun
A life not destroyed

I'm loved by the moon
I'm cradled by the black
My heart is back in tune
There is no turning back

And the stars are singing
A song I understand
I'm finally, finally
Reaching for your hand

[Instrumental Bridge]
*(Music swells. A long, beautiful, melodic, and highly emotional electric guitar solo. Full of hope, not rage.)*

*(Ethereal, layered vocals return)*
I see the colors of the nebula
A place I've only seen in dreams
The reds, the blues, the purples call
Flowing in majestic streams

This is the place I was meant to be
This is the "Stardust" that is me
The cosmos and the rocket's fire
My one, my only true desire

*(Ethereal, layered vocals)*
I'm loved by the sun
I'm held by the void
An orbit just begun
A life not destroyed

And the stars are singing
A song I understand
I'm finally, finally
Reaching for your hand

[Part IV: Re-entry (The New World)]
*(Music slowly fades back to just the shimmering synth pads and ethereal, whispered vocals)*

The walls are gone
The air is clear
There is no noise
There is no fear

The "Stardust" is a forgotten ghost
The "Engine" has delivered me to the coast
Of a new world, a new shore
A place I've never been before

The "Cage" is broken, the "Victim-Saint" is dead
A new, clean script is in my head
The "Friction" burned away in the fire
Leaving only my true desire

*(Ethereal, layered vocals swell for the finale)*
I'm loved by the sun...
I'm held by the void...
My orbit has begun...
My life is not destroyed...

Ad astra...
Ad astra...
(To the stars...)

*(Song fades out on a final, sustained, peaceful synthesizer chord)*

title: Neon Pulse
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Because the title track, *Midnight Drivers*, was the only song actually written during the rejected 1987 sessions, the band had to build an entire album around it from scratch in 2001. *Neon Pulse* serves as the retro-engineered prologue to that project. 

Written by Cassidy and Ryan in 2000, they deliberately designed the track to sound exactly like the pristine, high-speed synth-pop smash hits of the late 1980s. It represents the "before" picture—the vibrant, unburdened energy of the band driving into the city lights, completely unaware of the darkness that would follow. By opening the 2001 album with such a massive, era-accurate burst of energy, The Stardust Engine perfectly sets the stage for the sun to set, allowing the original 1987 Darkwave title track to hit with maximum impact on Side B.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(The track explodes out of the gate. A fast, driving drum machine locks in with a bright, arpeggiated analog synthesizer. Evan's bassline propels the momentum forward before Cassidy’s crystal-clear vocals cut through the mix.)*

[Verse 1]
The skyline wakes up when the sun goes down
A million electric eyes over the town
Turn the key and feel the hum in the floor
We’re leaving the shadows right outside the door

[Chorus]
Catch the neon pulse, riding on the wire
Racing through the streets, glowing like a fire
No looking back, we’re out of the frame
Running on the spark, wild and untamed
Catch the neon pulse tonight

[Verse 2]
The dashboard is glowing, a steady bright green
The fastest escape that this city has seen
The rhythm is beating right under our feet
A steady transmission out on the street

[Chorus]
Catch the neon pulse, riding on the wire
Racing through the streets, glowing like a fire
No looking back, we’re out of the frame
Running on the spark, wild and untamed
Catch the neon pulse tonight

[Bridge]
Through the tunnel lights, blinding and fast
Everything broken is left in the past
Hold on to the wheel, steady and sure
We’re chasing a frequency, perfect and pure

[Guitar Solo]
*(Ryan steps up, delivering a blistering, high-energy electric guitar solo that perfectly bridges the gap between Cassidy's retro synth-pop structure and the band's raw rock roots.)*

[Chorus]
Catch the neon pulse, riding on the wire
Racing through the streets, glowing like a fire
No looking back, we’re out of the frame
Running on the spark, wild and untamed
Catch the neon pulse tonight

[Outro]
Wild and untamed
Catch the pulse tonight
*(The synths swirl and peak as the instrumental slowly fades out into the city grid)*

title: Static on the Wire
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Continuing the retro-engineered illusion of Side A, *Static on the Wire* was crafted in 2001 to perfectly mimic the sterilized, radio-friendly synth-pop that Apex Records demanded in 1987. Cassidy and Ryan deliberately wrote the track to sound like a manufactured commercial hit—complete with a bright, sequenced synth hook and a driving dance beat. 

However, the detached, spoken-word bridge reveals the band's true identity bleeding through the pop facade. In the narrative context of the 2001 album, this track represents the band desperately "searching for a frequency" to connect with their true artistic voice amidst the corporate noise. It is a brilliant, catchy pop song that secretly acts as a critique of the 1980s music industry.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - A bright, highly sequenced analog synth hook drops in immediately, backed by a crisp, quantized drum machine.)*

[Verse 1]
Turning the dial, looking for a voice
In the static haze, we make a choice
A million signals crossing in the dark
Waiting for the one to ignite the spark

[Chorus]
Static on the wire, cutting through the night
Searching for a frequency, hiding out of sight
We're talking in the echoes, fading in the blue
Static on the wire, trying to get to you

[Verse 2]
The satellites are spinning overhead
Catching every word we never said
A broken transmission on a steady loop
We're caught inside the rhythm of the group

[Chorus]
Static on the wire, cutting through the night
Searching for a frequency, hiding out of sight
We're talking in the echoes, fading in the blue
Static on the wire, trying to get to you

[Bridge]
*(The melodic synths drop out, leaving a stark, pulsing bassline. The vocals shift into a detached, almost robotic spoken-word style, contrasting sharply with the pop energy of the rest of the track.)*
Signal lost.
Signal found.
Keep your feet on the ground.
Turn it up.
Break the sound.

[Synth Solo]
*(Instrumental - Cassidy takes the spotlight with a fast, arpeggiated synthesizer solo that cascades over the steady dance beat.)*

[Chorus]
Static on the wire, cutting through the night
Searching for a frequency, hiding out of sight
We're talking in the echoes, fading in the blue
Static on the wire, trying to get to you

[Outro]
Trying to get to you
Static on the wire
*(The sequenced hook repeats as the instrumental slowly fades out like a lost transmission)*

title: Concrete Angels
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
As the third track on Side A, *Concrete Angels* represents the massive, stadium-ready rock anthems that dominated the late 1980s. Written in 2001 specifically for the *Midnight Drivers* project, Ryan and Cassidy deliberately engineered this track to sound like a pristine, arena-shaking blockbuster that Apex Records would have eagerly pushed to rock radio in 1987. 

With Ryan taking over lead vocals, the song leans heavily into the band's raw rock roots while maintaining the polished, synth-heavy production of the era. To achieve this, the band relied heavily on Tyler's "Human Metronome" timing, anchoring the track with an explosive, quintessential 80s gated-reverb snare drum. Lyrically, while it masquerades as a standard driving anthem, it acts as a subtle nod to the band's survival—finding a sanctuary made of "stone and steel" and keeping the engine running on the inside, despite the heavy pressure of the corporate machine.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - The track opens with a massive, echoing 1980s gated-reverb snare drum. Evan's driving bassline locks in immediately before Ryan’s soaring electric guitar introduces the main melodic hook.)*

[Verse 1]
We built this sanctuary out of stone and steel
Looking for a frequency that we could actually feel
The grid is heavy but the signal’s running clear
We're standing on the edge, and there is nothing left to fear

[Chorus]
Concrete angels in the midnight rain
Riding out the storm on a moving train
We don't need a map, we don't need a guide
We've got the engine running on the inside
Concrete angels, taking flight

[Verse 2]
The overpass is glowing like a halo in the dark
Every single shadow is a place to leave a mark
We keep the rhythm steady, we keep the baseline low
Got a thousand miles behind us, and a thousand more to go

[Chorus]
Concrete angels in the midnight rain
Riding out the storm on a moving train
We don't need a map, we don't need a guide
We've got the engine running on the inside
Concrete angels, taking flight

[Bridge]
*(The instrumentation drops back, leaving just the thundering drums and Ryan's vocals to build the tension back up for the finale.)*
Hold the line
Keep the pace
Find the light in the empty space

[Guitar Solo]
*(Instrumental - Ryan unleashes a triumphant, stadium-sized electric guitar solo. It is highly melodic, dripping with chorus and delay, perfectly designed to bounce off the back walls of an arena.)*

[Chorus]
Concrete angels in the midnight rain
Riding out the storm on a moving train
We don't need a map, we don't need a guide
We've got the engine running on the inside
Concrete angels, taking flight

[Outro]
Running on the inside
Taking flight
*(The heavy drums crash one final time as the instrumental slowly fades out into the rain)*

title: The Overpass
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
If *Concrete Angels* was Ryan’s roaring stadium showcase, *The Overpass* belongs entirely to Evan Wright. Constructed during the 2001 sessions, this track was designed to replicate the driving, bass-heavy momentum of 80s New Wave deep cuts. 

In the fictionalized narrative of the "lost" 1987 album, this is the track that the hardcore fans (The Ironheads) would point to as proof of the band's underlying musicianship beneath the corporate pop sheen. Evan’s melodic, driving bass guitar sits front and center in the mix, acting as the primary engine of the song while Ryan delivers a tense, high-speed vocal performance. It perfectly captures the feeling of looking down at the gridlock of the 1980s music industry while cruising at top speed above it.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - Evan's driving, melodic bassline starts the track completely solo. Tyler's tight, gated snare drops in, setting a fast, aggressive highway tempo before bright analog synths wash over the rhythm.)*

[Verse 1]
Look at the gridlock bleeding in red
A million lost souls with a heavy tread
But we caught the ramp and we took the flight
Now we're cutting a line right through the night

[Chorus]
Up on the overpass, elevated and free
Leaving the ghosts in the machinery
A blur of neon and a steady hum
We're beating the rhythm of a different drum
Up on the overpass, watch us fly

[Verse 2]
The concrete ribbon is a perfect track
Got the pedal down and we're not looking back
Hear the frequency rising from the street below
But we're riding a current that they’ll never know

[Chorus]
Up on the overpass, elevated and free
Leaving the ghosts in the machinery
A blur of neon and a steady hum
We're beating the rhythm of a different drum
Up on the overpass, watch us fly

[Bridge]
Suspended in the air
Without a single care
The altitude is climbing
Perfect in our timing

[Guitar Solo]
*(Instrumental - Ryan delivers a sharp, staccato rock solo that weaves perfectly in and out of Evan's relentless bass groove.)*

[Chorus]
Up on the overpass, elevated and free
Leaving the ghosts in the machinery
A blur of neon and a steady hum
We're beating the rhythm of a different drum
Up on the overpass, watch us fly

[Outro]
High above the grid
Watch us fly
*(The synths fade out, leaving Evan's bassline to carry the final few seconds before abruptly stopping)*

title: The Horizon Line
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Serving as the grand finale to Side A, *The Horizon Line* was engineered in 2001 to act as the exact moment the "sun sets" on the band's bright, 1980s corporate pop era. It is the narrative bridge that connects the high-energy city lights of the first half to the isolating Darkwave reality of Side B.

To capture the emotional weight of this transition, Ryan and Cassidy utilized a rare, sweeping sibling duet. Their harmonies—honed over a lifetime of non-verbal communication—anchor a massive, hopeful crescendo of analog synth brass. However, rather than resolving the chord progression, the band intentionally cuts the track off abruptly. This jarring, unresolved silence was designed to physically force the listener into the quiet tension of flipping the vinyl record over, perfectly setting the stage for the original 1987 title track waiting in the dark.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - The track begins with a slow, building synth brass section. The tempo is deliberate and sweeping, signaling a major shift from the high-speed rock of the previous tracks.)*

[Verse 1]
The sky is turning violet and gold
The greatest story that we ever told
We're chasing down the edge of the light
Before the city gives into the night

[Chorus]
Out on the horizon line
Where the earth and the heavens align
We are running out of time
Out on the horizon line

[Verse 2]
The analog is fading away
We're leaving behind the static of the day
The engine hums a frequency clear
The final destination is near

[Chorus]
Out on the horizon line
Where the earth and the heavens align
We are running out of time
Out on the horizon line

[Bridge]
*(The rhythm section drops away entirely. Ryan and Cassidy's harmonized vocals float in the empty space before the final build.)*
The colors bleed, the shadows fall
We're standing at the edge of it all
We hold the note, we hold the breath
There is nothing left

[Synth Brass Solo]
*(Instrumental - A soaring, massive crescendo. Cassidy layers multiple tracks of synth brass over Tyler's thundering toms, creating a towering wall of sound.)*

[Chorus]
Out on the horizon line
Where the earth and the heavens align
We are running out of time
Out on the horizon line

[Outro]
The horizon line
The fading light
*(The massive instrumental abruptly stops on a lingering, unresolved chord, leaving behind absolute silence)*

title: Midnight Drivers
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
This is the artifact. *Midnight Drivers* is the singular, original track actually written and recorded during the fraught 1987 studio sessions. Apex Records executives famously halted production upon hearing it, deeming the track far too dark, moody, and atmospheric for their manufactured pop stars, ultimately shelving the entire album. 

When the band finally won their financial and creative independence from Apex in 1992, this track sat in the vault. It wasn't until the monumental success of the 1997 *Hard Reset* double-album that The Stardust Engine finally had the bandwidth to revisit it. In 2001, they built an entire retro-engineered 1980s New Wave album from scratch just to give this one song a conceptual home. Crucially, because this was recorded in 1987—three years prior to the crash that caused Ryan's T-10 paralysis—the band explicitly chose not to rework or re-record any of the stems. The 2001 release uses the raw, untouched 1987 master, preserving the exact darkwave energy of that era. It opens Side B, pulling the listener out of the city lights and into the isolating sanctuary of the night.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - A deep, pulsing synthesizer bassline cuts through the silence, anchored by a sparse, echoing drum machine that mimics the hypnotic rhythm of windshield wipers.)*

[Verse 1]
Headlights cut across the rain
Wipers keep the beat on the window pane
Static on the radio, fading in and out
We're just shadows in the dashboard lights

[Chorus]
Driving in the dark, running on neon fumes
Leaving all the ghosts in their empty rooms
This city's just a map of glowing, faded lines
But I'm safe right here, between your eyes and mine
Midnight drivers, in the fading light

[Verse 2]
The skyline's a promise, cold and blue
I don't know this road, but I'm with you
The analog display is a steady green glow
Got no destination, just a place to go

[Chorus]
Driving in the dark, running on neon fumes
Leaving all the ghosts in their empty rooms
This city's just a map of glowing, faded lines
But I'm safe right here, between your eyes and mine
Midnight drivers, in the fading light

[Bridge]
*(The beat drops out, leaving only an ethereal, sweeping synth pad. The vocals become intimate and quiet.)*
Don't have to talk
Don't have to say a thing
The engine hums a rhythm
The future's what we bring

[Synth Solo]
*(Instrumental - Cassidy delivers a moody, atmospheric synthesizer solo that weaves through the heavy reverb of the drum track.)*

[Chorus]
Driving in the dark, running on neon fumes
Leaving all the ghosts in their empty rooms
This city's just a map of glowing, faded lines
But I'm safe right here, between your eyes and mine
Midnight drivers, in the fading light

[Outro]
*(Instrumental fade-out with a heavy, distinct echo on the snare drum, trailing off into the dark)*

title: Shatterproof
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Following the dark, pulsing anchor of the 1987 title track, *Shatterproof* plunges the listener deeper into the isolating atmosphere of Side B. Written and recorded in 2001, the band designed this track to emulate the cold, European industrial Darkwave sound that was gaining underground traction in the late 1980s—the exact sound Apex Records actively feared.

Ryan steps up to the mic, but completely strips away his roaring, stadium-rock "Engine" persona. Instead, he delivers a detached, almost robotic vocal performance over a bed of icy keyboard pads and a sparse, mechanical drum machine. The result is a chilling, claustrophobic track about emotional lockdown and absolute isolation, perfectly sustaining the tension of the record's "Night Shift."

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - The track opens with a sparse, mechanical drum machine beat. Cold, analog synthesizer pads slowly wash in, creating an icy, unsettling atmosphere before Ryan's detached vocals begin.)*

[Verse 1]
The temperature drops in the empty room
A digital pulse in the silent gloom
I am watching the screen for a sign of life
Cutting the wire with a sterile knife

[Chorus]
Shatterproof
Behind the glass, I'm watching you move
Shatterproof
Nothing to break, nothing to prove
Cold to the touch and perfectly smooth
Shatterproof

[Verse 2]
The surface is clean and the edges are tight
I am locking the doors for the rest of the night
No static, no signal, no interference
Just the absolute zero of my appearance

[Chorus]
Shatterproof
Behind the glass, I'm watching you move
Shatterproof
Nothing to break, nothing to prove
Cold to the touch and perfectly smooth
Shatterproof

[Bridge]
*(The mechanical beat remains rigid as the synthesizer layers begin to stack, simulating a rising, cold pressure.)*
The pressure is rising
But the structure will hold
The circuitry freezing
Out in the cold

[Synth Solo]
*(Instrumental - Cassidy delivers a sharp, staccato, and icy synthesizer solo that feels precise and entirely devoid of human warmth.)*

[Chorus]
Shatterproof
Behind the glass, I'm watching you move
Shatterproof
Nothing to break, nothing to prove
Cold to the touch and perfectly smooth
Shatterproof

[Outro]
Perfectly smooth
Shatterproof
*(The rigid drum machine cuts out, and the icy synth pads slowly fade into the dark)*

title: Terminal Velvet
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
*Terminal Velvet* is the atmospheric centerpiece of Side B, and in the meta-narrative of the *Midnight Drivers* project, it is a fascinating chronological paradox. 

When recording the album in 2001, Cassidy O'Connell fully deployed her signature audio architecture: **The Cosmic Tidal Lock Sound™**. Because the album is presented as a "lost" 1987 record, Cassidy effectively used her 2001 engineering mastery to create a fictional "prototype" of the sound that wouldn't actually be perfected in reality until 1997's *Moon 1 (Tidal Lock)*. 

The result is a brooding, zero-gravity space-rock ballad that completely abandons the driving drum machines of Side A. Featuring her crystal-clear vocals suspended in a massive, mathematically precise web of lush spatial reverb, the track perfectly captures the sensation of drifting untethered into the endless black. (And while the in-universe lore proudly states Holly legally weaponized and trademarked this sonic architecture in 1997, the true magic simply lies in Cassidy's meticulous layering).

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - The track opens with a vast, echoing emptiness. A slow, melodic fretless bassline drifts into the stereo field, surrounded by shimmering, celestial analog synthesizers.)*

[Verse 1]
The atmosphere is thinning out tonight
I'm drifting past the edge of the satellite
The velvet curtain falling on the glass
I watch the constellations slowly pass

[Chorus]
Terminal velvet, painted in the black
We crossed the line and we can't go back
Caught in the orbit, losing the ground
Floating away in the tidal sound
Terminal velvet

[Verse 2]
The telemetry is quiet, the screen is dark
Just a fading memory of the final spark
The gravity is losing its heavy hold
Out in the beautiful, silent cold

[Chorus]
Terminal velvet, painted in the black
We crossed the line and we can't go back
Caught in the orbit, losing the ground
Floating away in the tidal sound
Terminal velvet

[Bridge]
*(The rhythm section drops away entirely. Cassidy's vocals are layered with heavy, pristine reverb, floating freely in the mix without any anchor.)*
No friction to slow us down
No gravity to pull us to the ground
Just the endless empty space
A perfect, frozen state of grace

[Synth Solo]
*(Instrumental - A lush, sweeping synthesizer solo that glides effortlessly, mimicking the slow rotation of a station in deep space.)*

[Chorus]
Terminal velvet, painted in the black
We crossed the line and we can't go back
Caught in the orbit, losing the ground
Floating away in the tidal sound
Terminal velvet

[Outro]
Floating away
Terminal velvet
*(The atmospheric pads slowly dissipate, leaving a final, lingering echo that fades entirely into the void)*

title: Digital Ghosts
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
As the penultimate track of the core album, *Digital Ghosts* strips The Stardust Engine down to its absolute bare minimum. Engineered in 2001 to simulate the sterile, early digital recording environments of the late 1980s, the band completely removes Evan's driving bass guitar and reduces Tyler's massive drum kit to a solitary, echoing click track. 

The result is a haunting, ethereal duet between Ryan and Cassidy. Lyrically, it explores the existential dread of being erased by the corporate machine—becoming nothing more than "ghosts in the mainframe" of a label like Apex Records. The minimalist analog synthesizers and heavy spatial reverb create a vast, empty soundscape where the siblings' harmonies drift like lost signals, perfectly capturing the isolation of Side B's "Night Shift" before the sun finally rises.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - A slow, atmospheric synthesizer pad swells into the void, anchored only by a sparse, echoing click track that mimics a failing digital heartbeat.)*

[Verse 1]
There is a shadow in the mainframe tonight
A silhouette trapped in the fading light
I hear a whisper in the empty wire
The cooling ashes of a digital fire

[Chorus]
We are just ghosts in the machine
Living in the spaces in between
Echoes bouncing off the satellite
Fading out of focus, out of sight
Digital ghosts

[Verse 2]
The memory banks are slowly wiping clean
Erasing every place that we have been
But I can feel you in the static hum
A quiet rhythm from a distant drum

[Chorus]
We are just ghosts in the machine
Living in the spaces in between
Echoes bouncing off the satellite
Fading out of focus, out of sight
Digital ghosts

[Bridge]
*(The sparse click track fades back, leaving the harmonized vocals suspended in a wash of heavy spatial reverb.)*
Drifting through the code
On a silent road
Nothing left to save
Just a quiet wave

[Synth Solo]
*(Instrumental - Cassidy delivers a melancholic, echoing analog synthesizer solo that drifts aimlessly through the sparse mix, sounding like a corrupted data file.)*

[Chorus]
We are just ghosts in the machine
Living in the spaces in between
Echoes bouncing off the satellite
Fading out of focus, out of sight
Digital ghosts

[Outro]
Out of focus
Out of sight
*(The synths slowly dissolve, leaving only the fading click track before it drops into absolute silence)*

title: Fade to Dawn
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Serving as the epic 6-minute finale to the core album, *Fade to Dawn* is proudly cited by both Ryan and Cassidy as their absolute favorite track on the *Midnight Drivers* project. 

Engineered in 2001 to act as the ultimate cathartic release for the "lost" 1987 record, this track masterfully bridges the two halves of the album. It takes the brooding, atmospheric Darkwave tension of Side B and slowly morphs it back into the triumphant, stadium-shaking Arena Rock of Side A. The song is a slow burn, opening with nearly a full minute of progressive instrumental tension before Cassidy's ethereal vocals even enter the mix. When Ryan's guitar finally shatters the darkwave atmosphere with a massive, wide-panned solo, it represents the sun finally breaking over the horizon. The band survived the night shift, and they made it through the dark together.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - The track begins with a masterclass in slow-burn progression. For a full 50 seconds, a solitary synth and a steady drum heartbeat slowly build tension in the dark. At the 0:50 mark, Cassidy’s ethereal, wordless vocalizations drift over the mix like the first hint of morning light.)*

[Verse 1]
*(At 1:03, the tension breaks. Ryan and Cassidy enter together in a unified, sweeping duet.)*
The darkest hour is heavy and cold
The final chapter of the story we told
The dashboard fades from green to grey
The neon signs are washing away

[Chorus]
Fade to dawn
The night is breaking and the ghosts are gone
Keep the engine running, keep the rhythm drawn
We made it through the dark
Now we fade to dawn

[Verse 2]
There is a crack in the velvet sky
A silver lining bleeding into the eye
The tires hum a steady, tired song
We held the line all night long

[Chorus]
Fade to dawn
The night is breaking and the ghosts are gone
Keep the engine running, keep the rhythm drawn
We made it through the dark
Now we fade to dawn

[Bridge]
*(The synths pull back as the rhythm section begins a heavy, steady climb toward the climax.)*
The pressure drops
The signal stops
The shadows run
Here comes the sun

[Guitar and Synth Solo]
*(Instrumental - Ryan unleashes an absolutely massive, soaring stadium-rock guitar solo. Dripping with atmospheric delay and chorus, it evokes the legendary, wide-panned tones of the late 80s, completely shattering the darkwave isolation and flooding the track with pure rock catharsis.)*

[Chorus]
Fade to dawn
The night is breaking and the ghosts are gone
Keep the engine running, keep the rhythm drawn
We made it through the dark
Now we fade to dawn

[Outro]
We made it through the dark
Fade to dawn
*(The massive wall of sound slowly washes away, fading out into the bright, warm light of morning)*

title: Midnight Drivers (Basement Demo)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Included as an archival bonus track on the 2001 CD release, this is the absolute genesis of the *Midnight Drivers* concept. Recorded on a handheld cassette deck in 1986, this raw, unpolished demo features just Cassidy on an upright piano in their parents' basement while Ryan works out the vocal melody. 

To ensure strict compliance with modern digital distribution platforms and avoid automated metadata rejections, Engine Room Records officially cataloged the track simply as "Midnight Drivers (Basement Demo)," cleanly sidestepping any systemic confusion over its 1986 recording date versus its 2026 digital upload date. It strips away the heavy Darkwave production of the studio master, revealing the intimate, familial songwriting core that the band would ultimately build their empire upon.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - Soft, slightly muffled upright piano chords with noticeable room ambiance and the faint hiss of cassette tape.)*

[Verse 1]
Headlights cut across the rain
Wipers keep the beat on the window pane
Static on the radio, fading in and out
We're just shadows in the dashboard lights

[Chorus]
Driving in the dark, running on neon fumes
Leaving all the ghosts in their empty rooms
This city's just a map of glowing, faded lines
But I'm safe right here, between your eyes and mine
Midnight drivers, in the fading light

[Verse 2]
The skyline's a promise, cold and blue
I don't know this road, but I'm with you
The analog display is a steady green glow
Got no destination, just a place to go

[Chorus]
Driving in the dark, running on neon fumes
Leaving all the ghosts in their empty rooms
This city's just a map of glowing, faded lines
But I'm safe right here, between your eyes and mine
Midnight drivers, in the fading light

[Outro]
In the fading light
*(The acoustic piano slows down and fades out slowly into the tape hiss)*

title: Neon Pulse (12" Extended Club Mix)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
To fully sell the illusion that *Midnight Drivers* was a complete, commercially prepared 1987 release before being shelved, Engine Room Records included this "rare" 12-inch extended club mix on the 2001 CD reissue. 

In the 1980s, labels like Apex Records would regularly press these extended, 5-minute remixes onto heavy 12-inch vinyl and distribute them exclusively to underground club DJs in New York and London. The wider vinyl grooves allowed for massive, bone-rattling bass response. Engineered in 2001 to perfectly replicate this era, the track deconstructs the tight, radio-friendly structure of the original *Neon Pulse*. It stretches the arrangement into a hypnotic, driving groove, replacing the traditional guitar solo with a heavy, four-on-the-floor instrumental dance break designed entirely to keep the dance floor moving.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(Instrumental - Unlike the radio edit, the track opens with a long, 60-second driving drum beat and pulsing synth bassline. It is designed to give club DJs plenty of time to beatmatch and blend the track into their live sets before the vocals finally arrive.)*

[Verse 1]
The skyline wakes up when the sun goes down
A million electric eyes over the town
Turn the key and feel the hum in the floor
We’re leaving the shadows right outside the door

[Chorus]
Catch the neon pulse, riding on the wire
Racing through the streets, glowing like a fire
No looking back, we’re out of the frame
Running on the spark, wild and untamed
Catch the neon pulse tonight

[Verse 2]
The dashboard is glowing, a steady bright green
The fastest escape that this city has seen
The rhythm is beating right under our feet
A steady transmission out on the street

[Chorus]
Catch the neon pulse, riding on the wire
Racing through the streets, glowing like a fire
No looking back, we’re out of the frame
Running on the spark, wild and untamed
Catch the neon pulse tonight

[Bridge]
*(The heavy beat drops out entirely, leaving just the atmospheric synths to build the tension in the room.)*
Through the tunnel lights, blinding and fast
Everything broken is left in the past
Hold on to the wheel, steady and sure
We’re chasing a frequency, perfect and pure

[Guitar Solo]
*(Instrumental - extended dance break. Instead of a traditional guitar solo, the heavy four-on-the-floor beat slams back in. The track locks into a hypnotic, driving instrumental groove that stretches on, letting the rhythm section completely take over the mix.)*

[Chorus]
Catch the neon pulse, riding on the wire
Racing through the streets, glowing like a fire
No looking back, we’re out of the frame
Running on the spark, wild and untamed
Catch the neon pulse tonight

[Outro]
Wild and untamed
Catch the pulse tonight
*(The vocals echo out, leaving the driving drum machine to carry the track through a long, DJ-friendly instrumental fade-out)*

title: Static on the Wire (Instrumental B-Side)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
To fully commit to the illusion of a lost 1987 record, Engine Room Records included this "Instrumental B-Side" on the 2001 CD release. In the 1980s, instrumental versions of hit singles were famously pressed on the flip sides of 7-inch 45 RPM vinyl records. By stripping away the vocals, this bonus track allows listeners to fully appreciate the meticulous, era-accurate synthesizer programming and drum machine loops the band engineered in 2001 to flawlessly recreate the sound of their corporate past.

**LYRICS:**

[Instrumental]
*(The track is identical to the album version of "Static on the Wire," completely devoid of vocal tracks, allowing the bright, sequenced synth hook and driving dance beat to carry the entire arrangement.)*

title: The Engine Room (Studio Jam)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Closing out the 2001 CD reissue is this raw, gritty instrumental outtake. To complete the illusion of the 1987 recording sessions, Engine Room Records included this "leaked" studio jam session featuring only the band's rhythm section: identical twins Evan (bass) and Tyler (drums).

According to the lore, Ryan and Cassidy had stepped out of the booth, and the twins locked the soundproof doors to keep everyone—including any hallucinated arena crowds—outside. With the tape left rolling, they laid down a blistering, unpolished groove. Devoid of any synthesizers or soaring vocals, this track puts the spotlight entirely on Tyler's heavy gated-reverb snare and Evan's driving, distorted bassline. It is pure, unfiltered rhythm section muscle, proving that even without the "Stardust," the engine still roars.

**LYRICS:**

*(As a purely instrumental jam session, there are no traditional lyrics. The following structural blueprint represents the exact musical cues Evan and Tyler followed in the soundproof room to build the track.)*

[Intro]
[Heavy Four-on-the-Floor Drum Beat]
[Slow Bass Slide]

[Verse]
[Driving Melodic Bassline]
[Steady Hi-Hat Groove]

[Chorus]
[Massive Gated Reverb Snare]
[Aggressive Slap Bass]

[Bridge]
[Drum Fill]
[Syncopated Rhythm]
[Stripped Down Bass]

[Guitar Solo - Bass Only]
[Heavy Distortion Bass]

[Outro]
[Drum Crescendo]
[Abrupt Stop]

title: Moon 2 (Roche Limit)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
Exploding out of the gate as the opening track for *Mile Marker 98*, this is the spark that ignited Ryan's 2003 solo orbit. Freed from the need to balance the "Stardust" with his "Engine," Ryan takes the astrophysical concept of the Roche limit—the exact point gravity tears a moon to pieces—and uses it to set the destructive, high-friction tone for the entire record. It serves as a violent, visceral thesis statement for his confrontation with the trauma of the "Crash of '90", introducing blistering, distorted electric guitars and pure, aggressive rock energy right from the first second. 

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(An aggressive, pounding 1980s drum fill shatters the ambient space. A heavy, distorted electric guitar riff locks in with a driving analog synth-bass. It is high energy, frantic, and immediately dangerous.)*

[Verse 1]
I see the warning on the console screen
You're pulling harder than I've ever seen
I'm caught inside the gravity well
A one-way ticket on a carousel
The navigation is totally blind
I'm leaving all the safety behind

[Pre-Chorus]
The structural integrity is starting to bend
I'm accelerating faster to the bitter end
You're a massive body, I'm a satellite
I'm breaking into pieces in the dead of night!

[Chorus]
We crossed the Roche Limit!
Tearing me apart, tearing me apart!
We crossed the Roche Limit!
A fatal attraction, a heavy heart!
There's no surviving when you get this close
A total system failure, a lethal dose
Yeah, we crossed the Roche Limit!

[Verse 2]
The telemetry is spinning into the red
I should've listened to the things they said
But the magnetic field is pulling me in
I feel the friction underneath my skin
I'm caught in the orbit of a dangerous star
I didn't mean to take it this far!

[Pre-Chorus]
The structural integrity is starting to bend
I'm accelerating faster to the bitter end
You're a massive body, I'm a satellite
I'm breaking into pieces in the dead of night!

[Chorus]
We crossed the Roche Limit!
Tearing me apart, tearing me apart!
We crossed the Roche Limit!
A fatal attraction, a heavy heart!
There's no surviving when you get this close
A total system failure, a lethal dose
Yeah, we crossed the Roche Limit!

[Guitar Solo]
*(Ryan unleashes a blisteringly fast, aggressive, face-melting electric guitar solo. Explosive drums and heavy synth pads pulse underneath the shredding, simulating a vessel vibrating itself to pieces under extreme gravitational stress.)*

[Chorus]
We crossed the Roche Limit!
Tearing me apart, tearing me apart!
We crossed the Roche Limit!
A fatal attraction, a heavy heart!
There's no surviving when you get this close
A total system failure, a lethal dose
Yeah, we crossed the Roche Limit!

[Outro]
Tearing me apart.
System failure.
*(A final, heavy guitar chord rings out, turning into a wall of amplifier feedback that slowly fades out into the cold, indifferent silence of deep space)*

title: Moon 1 (Tidal Lock)
content:
**LORE NOTE:**
As the opening track of her 2003 solo orbit, *Sanctuary (Zero-G)*, "Moon 1 (Tidal Lock)" expands from a single standout deep-cut into the foundational thesis of Cassidy's entire musical architecture. This is the ultimate, undiluted presentation of **The Cosmic Tidal Lock Sound™**. By kicking off the album with this 1980s Cosmic Pop masterpiece, she establishes the zero-gravity environment for the rest of the record—a sanctuary built on unwavering loyalty, sweeping spatial reverb, and mathematically perfect analog synthesizers. It is the sound of the "Anchor" finding her perfect, peaceful center of gravity.

**LYRICS:**

[Intro]
*(A tight, driving 1980s drum beat enters. A pulsing synth bassline locks into the rhythm, perfectly anchoring the track as shimmering analog synth pads slowly rise in the background.)*

[Verse 1]
We crossed the line into the dark
Left the atmosphere, a fading spark
But I can feel the pull of where you are
A heavy gravity from a distant star
They tried to spin us out of control
But we found the center, we found the soul

[Pre-Chorus]
The telemetry is clear tonight
Half in the shadow, half in the light
I'm caught in the orbit, spinning around
A million miles above the ground

[Chorus]
And I'm in a tidal lock with you!
Suspended in the velvet and the blue
No matter how I turn, no matter where I fly
You are the only anchor in my sky
Yeah, I'm in a tidal lock with you!

[Verse 2]
The radio is quiet, the signal is dead
But I hear the rhythm inside my head
We don't need the sun to show us the way
We left the noise of the terrestrial day
We're charting the math that they couldn't see
Perfect alignment in the zero-G

[Pre-Chorus]
The telemetry is clear tonight
Half in the shadow, half in the light
I'm caught in the orbit, spinning around
A million miles above the ground

[Chorus]
And I'm in a tidal lock with you!
Suspended in the velvet and the blue
No matter how I turn, no matter where I fly
You are the only anchor in my sky
Yeah, I'm in a tidal lock with you!

[Instrumental Spacewalk]
*(Evan steps into the spotlight, playing a melodic, sliding fretless bass solo over Tyler's driving drum beat. Cassidy's analog synths sparkle in the background like passing satellites, creating a lush, unmistakably 80s soundscape.)*

[Chorus]
'Cause I'm in a tidal lock with you!
Suspended in the velvet and the blue
No matter how I turn, no matter where I fly
You are the only anchor in my sky
Yeah, I'm in a tidal lock with you!

[Outro]
Half in the shadow.
Half in the light.
Tidal lock.
*(The synths sweep upward, echoing out into the void as the driving drum beat slowly fades away into the starfield)*

title: Overture of the Frost
content:
[Instrumental Intro]
[Symphonic Strings]

[Heavy Cello Build]
[Rock Drum Beat]

[Distorted Guitar Entrance]
[Epic Orchestral Drop]

[Choir]
Ahhh, Ohhh
Ahhh, Ohhh

[Massive Symphonic Rock Climax]
[Heavy Rhythm Section]

[Sudden Stop]
[Lonely Piano Outro]
[Fade to Silence]

title: The False Horizon
content:
[Verse 1]
Rolling south down Hampton line
Tracing the grid inside the mind
The hospital glass is coming near
The transfer point is almost here
The air brakes hiss, the yellow cord is pulled
Following the rules, just like we were told

[Pre-Chorus]
A sudden hand upon the arm
A sweet smile to disarm the alarm
We're staying on, skipping the door
There's a little something more

[Chorus]
Look at the false horizon!
A detour in the master plan!
The curfew clock begins to fade
Just trying to play the easy hand
Ignore the warnings in the chest
For a spontaneous request
Swallow the panic, take the lead
Into the emerald greed!

[Verse 2]
Olney and Granby, stepping in the cold
This isn't the story we were told
A concrete fortress standing tall
The diesel fumes, the echoing hall
Wayfarer silver, Pathfinder blue
What exactly are we going to do?

[Bridge]
Two tickets out! Next bus to ride!
Richmond is waiting on the other side!
The borders break, the tether tears!
The armor's left upon the stairs!

[Outro]
A sudden twist, an outstretched hand
Walking blind into the sand
A quiet nod, the closing eyes
The heavy gate locks on the prize!
(The trap locks shut!)
(The trap locks shut!)

[Sudden Heavy Orchestral Hit]
[End]

title: Emerald City (The Trigger)
content:
[Verse 1]
Looking through the rain-streaked glass
Waiting for the gray to pass
They say there's a place where the colors shine
Somewhere past the border line
A quiet dream above the rain
To wash away the miles of pain

[Pre-Chorus]
But the sky turns black and the tires whine
Welcome to the end of the line

[Chorus]
The neon burns and the skyline bleeds!
Trapped inside the emerald greed!
The sepia fades to a blinding light!
Swallowed by the city tonight!
There is no magic at the end of the road!
Just a heavy, crushing load!

[Verse 2]
I watched the gray world disappear
But there is only terror here
The towering glass and the glowing green
Waking up inside the machine
The walls are high and the gates are locked
With every single hour mocked

[Bridge]
I can't go back, the bridge is burned!
Every single lesson learned!
The vibrant colors suffocate!
Driven by a silent fate!

[Outro]
I just want to go home
I just want to go home
The emerald light is blinding me
I just want to go home
I just want to go home

title: Upon This Winter's Night
content:
\[Intro]
(Sudden, heavy driving cello riff enters)
(Concussive rock drum kit and distorted rhythm guitar crash in)

\[Verse 1]
(symphonic rock music backs off to allow glockenspiel to take over the melody)
The clock upon the mantel chimes
Reminding me of better times
The window pane is frosted white
I'm searching for a sign tonight

\[Pre-Chorus]
(Strings build tension, symphonic rock slowly kicks back in)
Can you hear me calling?
Can you see the glow?
Through the miles of silence
And the drifting snow?

\[Chorus]
(Symphonic Rock detonates with full authority back in at the word Candle)
So keep a candle burning bright
Upon this winter's night
Though you've wandered far and wide
Time remains upon your side
Follow the star, follow the light
And make it home tonight (Make it home tonight!)

\[Instrumental Break]
(Dramatic Cello Solo)

\[Verse 2]
The world is cold and full of fear
But sanctuary waits in here
Don't let the shadows steal your way
Don't listen to the words they say
The journey’s hard, the road is steep
But promises are yours to keep
So lift your head and face the wind
And let the journey home begin

\[Chorus]
So keep a candle burning bright
Upon this winter's night
Though you've wandered far and wide
Time remains upon your side
Follow the star, follow the light
And make it home tonight

\[Bridge]
(Tempo speeds up - Rock Opera style)
(Male Lead) I’m sending out a prayer!
(Choir) (Bring him home! Bring him home!)
(Male Lead) To vanish all despair!
(Choir) (You are not alone!)
(Male Lead) No matter where you are No matter how far!
(Male Lead) Just look to the Northern Star!

\[Guitar Solo]
(Soaring, melodic electric guitar solo with orchestral backing)

\[Chorus]
So keep a candle burning bright
Upon this winter's night
Though you've wandered far and wide
Time remains upon your side
Follow the star, follow the light
And make it home tonight

\[Outro]
Make it home tonight...
(Follow the light...)
Make it home tonight...
